section_title: Synthesis and Conclusion: Reassessing the Canonical VIP Disinhibitor

findings.claim: We show that in cortical layers 2/3 and 5 parvalbumin (Pvalb), somatostatin (Sst), and vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) expressing interneurons interact via a simple and complementary connectivity scheme.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that in cortical layers 2/3 and 5 parvalbumin (Pvalb), somatostatin (Sst), and vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) expressing interneurons interact via a simple and complementary connectivity scheme.
findings.evidence: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These three lines expressed Cre in three largely non-overlapping populations of interneurons that, together, represent approximately 80% of interneurons in primary visual cortex.

findings.claim: We identified a basic disinhibitory circuit module in which activation of VIP interneurons transiently suppresses primarily somatostatin- and a fraction of parvalbumin-expressing inhibitory interneurons that specialize in the control of the input and output of principal cells, respectively 3 , 6 , 16 , 17 .
findings.claim_source_sentence: We identified a basic disinhibitory circuit module in which activation of VIP interneurons transiently suppresses primarily somatostatin- and a fraction of parvalbumin-expressing inhibitory interneurons that specialize in the control of the input and output of principal cells, respectively 3 , 6 , 16 , 17 .
findings.evidence: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Indeed, VIP expression demarcates a small population of all interneurons (~10%), distinct from the two major interneuron populations defined by parvalbumin (Pv) and somatostatin (Som) expression 19 , 20 .

findings.claim: We found that the neural activity of VIP neurons in mouse V1 is closely correlated with the locomotion even without visual stimulation, when most other neurons in the visual cortex show only spontaneous activity.

findings.claim: We examined the visual responses of three non-overlapping GABAergic neuron subtypes based on their immunoreactivity for the markers parvalbumin (PV, 21% GABAergic neurons in V1 Layer 2/3), somatostatin (SOM, 8%), and vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP, 17%) (percentages from Xu et al., 2009 ), which correspond primarily to basket, Martinotti and bipolar morphologies, respectively ( Celio and Heizmann, 1981; Connor and Peters, 1984; Wahle, 1993; Kawaguchi and Kubota, 1997; Xu et al., 2006 ).

findings.claim: Using several Cre mouse lines, we performed cellular-resolution Ca 2+ imaging from excitatory pyramidal (PYR) neurons as well as three distinct subtypes of inhibitory interneurons: parvalbumin-positive (PV + ), somatostatin-positive (SST + ), and vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive (VIP + ) neurons, which together comprise 85% of all GABAergic neurons in the cortex ( Rudy et al., 2011; Xu et al., 2010 ).

findings.claim: Using simultaneous quadruple-octuple in vitro and dual in vivo whole-cell recordings, we found two previously unknown interneuronal circuits that link cortical layer 1-3 (L1-3) interneurons and L5 pyramidal neurons in the rat neocortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using simultaneous quadruple-octuple in vitro and dual in vivo whole-cell recordings, we found two previously unknown interneuronal circuits that link cortical layer 1-3 (L1-3) interneurons and L5 pyramidal neurons in the rat neocortex.

findings.claim: Recordings in brain slices revealed that in V1 L1 inhibitory neurons received the maximum amount of direct input from A1 as compared with other cell types across all the cortical layers, including parvalbumin (PV), somatostatin (SOM), and vasoactive intestine peptide (VIP) inhibitory neurons as well as pyramidal cells.

findings.claim: Modulation of firing by locomotion ( Ayaz et al., 2013; Erisken et al., 2014; Fu et al., 2014; Niell and Stryker, 2010 ) has been suggested to arise from a disinhibitory circuit, where interneurons expressing vasoactive intestinal peptide ( Vip ) inhibit Sst interneurons and thereby disinhibit pyramidal (Pyr) cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Modulation of firing by locomotion ( Ayaz et al., 2013; Erisken et al., 2014; Fu et al., 2014; Niell and Stryker, 2010 ) has been suggested to arise from a disinhibitory circuit, where interneurons expressing vasoactive intestinal peptide ( Vip ) inhibit Sst interneurons and thereby disinhibit pyramidal (Pyr) cells.

findings.claim: Using in vivo two-photon calcium imaging in layers 2/3 and 4 in mouse V1, we reveal that locomotion increases the activity of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using in vivo two-photon calcium imaging in layers 2/3 and 4 in mouse V1, we reveal that locomotion increases the activity of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model.
findings.evidence: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex

findings.claim:
VIP interneurons, themselves regulated by neuromodulators, may therefore enable selective patterns of activity to propagate through the cortex, by generating a "spotlight of attention".

Significance statement

Most inhibitory interneurons have axons restricted to a nearby area and target excitatory neighbors indiscriminately, raising the issue of how neuronal activity can propagate through cortical circuits.

findings.claim: Here we demonstrate that SOM neurons are active at a greater dynamic ACh range than VIP and L1 neurons, and cholinergic inputs to the superficial layers of primary visual cortex (V1) act via SOM neurons (but not VIP and L1 neurons) to activate a specific inhibitory-excitatory cortical circuit that drives alterations of brain state synchrony and neuronal correlations.

findings.claim:
These results establish that cortical inhibition modulates the spatial resolution of visual processing and add further evidence demonstrating that feature selectivity depends, not only on the feedforward excitatory projections into the cortex, but also on dynamic intracortical modulations by specific forms of inhibition.

Significance statement

We demonstrate that interneurons expressing vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP+) play a causal role in regulating the spatial frequency (SF) tuning of neurons in mouse visual cortex.

findings.claim: Since our lab and others have already demonstrated that the motor cortex sends long-range Parvalbumin- and Somatostatin-expressing axons to the dorsal striatum (Rock et al., 2016 ; Melzer et al., 2017 ), a cortical area involved not only in sensory processing but also in planned and motivated behavior, we asked whether long-range VIP neurons are specific for the auditory cortex or are a common feature of the corticofugal circuit organization.

(D) Summary plot of Pearson correlation coefficients between calcium activity and running speed.

findings.claim: However, it was recently shown that only half of VIP interneurons display a 5HT3a receptor response and a subset of VIP interneurons in visual cortex co‑express SST.
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, it was recently shown that only half of VIP interneurons display a 5HT3a receptor response and a subset of VIP interneurons in visual cortex co‑express SST.
findings.evidence: Updating the picture of layer 2/3 VIP-expressing interneuron function in the mouse cerebral cortex
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP interneurons in the neocortex account for roughly 12% of all interneurons.

findings.claim: Similar to what has been shown in the barrel cortex ( Prönneke et al., 2015 ; Almási et al., 2019 ) and visual cortex ( Gonchar et al., 2007 ), we found in the motor cortex that most VIP INs were located in L2/3 ( Supplementary Figure 3 ).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Similar to what has been shown in the barrel cortex ( Prönneke et al., 2015 ; Almási et al., 2019 ) and visual cortex ( Gonchar et al., 2007 ), we found in the motor cortex that most VIP INs were located in L2/3 ( Supplementary Figure 3 ).
findings.evidence: The spike-timing-dependent plasticity of VIP interneurons in motor cortex
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The solution was bubbled with 95% O 2 /5% CO 2 for 10 min and cooled on ice to ∼4°C.

findings.claim: Short-term depression at VIP IN→MC synapses was described in the motor cortex and the auditory and medial prefrontal cortices ( Pi et al., 2013 ), while short-term facilitation was described in the somatosensory cortex ( Walker et al., 2016 ) and visual cortex ( Campagnola et al., 2022 ).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Short-term depression at VIP IN→MC synapses was described in the motor cortex ( Figure 1F ) and the auditory and medial prefrontal cortices ( Pi et al., 2013 ), while short-term facilitation was described in the somatosensory cortex ( Walker et al., 2016 ) and visual cortex ( Campagnola et al., 2022 ).
findings.evidence: The short-term plasticity of VIP interneurons in motor cortex
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The solution was bubbled with 95% O 2 /5% CO 2 for 10 min and cooled on ice to ~4°C.

findings.claim: Among various classes of INs, vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing (VIP<sup>+</sup> ) INs preferentially target other INs in several brain regions and thereby directly modulate the GABAergic system.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Among various classes of INs, vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing (VIP<sup>+</sup> ) INs preferentially target other INs in several brain regions and thereby directly modulate the GABAergic system.
findings.evidence: Morpho-physiological properties and connectivity of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons in the mouse hippocampal dentate gyrus
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The remaining cells project their axons exclusively to the molecular layer (15%), to both the molecular layer and hilus (10%), or throughout the entire DG layers (8%).

findings.claim: Here we investigate this question, focusing on the cortical Vip-Sst disinhibitory circuit.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we investigate this question, focusing on the cortical Vip-Sst disinhibitory circuit.
findings.evidence: Behavioral strategy shapes activation of the Vip-Sst disinhibitory circuit in visual cortex

findings.claim: Abstract Aim Despite dysfunctional vasoactive intestinal polypeptide‐positive interneurons (VIP‐INs) being linked to the emergence of neurodevelopmental disorders, the temporal profile of VIP‐IN functional maturation and cortical network integration remains unclear.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Abstract Aim Despite dysfunctional vasoactive intestinal polypeptide‐positive interneurons (VIP‐INs) being linked to the emergence of neurodevelopmental disorders, the temporal profile of VIP‐IN functional maturation and cortical network integration remains unclear.
findings.evidence: Postnatal development of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing GABAergic interneurons in mouse somatosensory cortex
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Despite GABAergic INs only comprising about 15%–20% of all cerebral neurons [for review, see Staiger et al.

findings.claim: In contrast, as described earlier, a disinhibitory circuit comprised of VIP cells and SST cells plays a key role in control of the gain of visual responses and in facilitation of plasticity by locomotion in adult visual cortex, whereas PV neurons do not show consistent responses to locomotion .
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, as described earlier, a disinhibitory circuit comprised of VIP cells and SST cells plays a key role in control of the gain of visual responses and in facilitation of plasticity by locomotion in adult visual cortex, whereas PV neurons do not show consistent responses to locomotion ( 3 , 4 ).
findings.evidence: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The mouse was anesthetized with isoflurane (3% for induction and 0.7% during recording), supplemented with intramuscular injection of chlorprothixene chloride (2 µg/g body wt), and images were recorded transcranially through the window of the implanted headplate.

findings.claim: A candidate mechanism is the disinhibition of pyramidal cells through vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and somatostatin (SOM) positive interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A candidate mechanism is the disinhibition of pyramidal cells through vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and somatostatin (SOM) positive interneurons.
findings.evidence: Attentional modulation is orthogonal to disinhibition by VIP interneurons in primary visual cortex

findings.claim: 23060193 10.1038/nature11526 PMC3621107 Askew CE , Lopez AJ , Wood MA , Metherate R Nicotine excites VIP interneurons to disinhibit pyramidal neurons in auditory cortex .
findings.claim_source_sentence: 23060193 10.1038/nature11526 PMC3621107 Askew CE , Lopez AJ , Wood MA , Metherate R ( 2019 ) Nicotine excites VIP interneurons to disinhibit pyramidal neurons in auditory cortex .
findings.evidence: VIP interneurons regulate cortical size tuning and visual perception
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Cell type-specific expression of caspase caused rapid cell death in >75% of VIP-INs in V1 within two weeks of viral injection ( SFig.

findings.title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control.
findings.claim: VIP→SST/PV disinhibitory module in cortex is recruited by reward and punishment during behavior
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that a class of interneurons that express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) mediates disinhibitory control in multiple areas of neocortex and is recruited by reinforcement signals.
findings.citation: Pi HJ, Hangya B, Kvitsiani D, Sanders JI, Huang ZJ, Kepecs A.

findings.title: A cortical circuit for gain control by behavioral state.
findings.claim: Locomotion activates V1 VIP neurons via nicotinic basal-forebrain inputs and increases visual gain
findings.claim_source_sentence: In vivo calcium imaging of behaving animals revealed that locomotion activates vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-positive neurons in mouse V1 independent of visual stimulation and largely through nicotinic inputs from basal forebrain.
findings.citation: Fu Y, Tucciarone JM, Espinosa JS, Sheng N, Darcy DP, Nicoll RA, Huang ZJ, Stryker MP.

findings.title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex.
findings.claim: Whisker motor cortex (vM1) recruits VIP interneurons in S1, disinhibiting pyramidal cells during whisking
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that S1-projecting vM1 pyramidal neurons strongly recruited vasointestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing GABAergic interneurons, a subset of serotonin receptor-expressing interneurons.
findings.citation: Lee S, Kruglikov I, Huang ZJ, Fishell G, Rudy B, Rudy B.

findings.title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior.
findings.claim: In dmPFC during a sensory-discrimination task, VIP interneurons respond strongly to action outcomes
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that inhibitory interneurons of the same subtype were similar to each other, but different subtypes preferentially signaled different task-related events: somatostatin-positive neurons primarily signaled motor action (licking), vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive neurons responded strongly to action outcomes, whereas parvalbumin-positive neurons were less selective, responding to sensory cues, motor action, and trial outcomes.
findings.citation: Pinto L, Dan Y.

findings.title: Cross-Modality Sharpening of Visual Cortical Processing through Layer-1-Mediated Inhibition and Disinhibition.
findings.claim: Sound suppresses V1 L2/3 VIP cells preferentially at preferred orientation, contributing to cross-modal sharpening of orientation tuning
findings.claim_source_sentence: L1 neurons were strongly innervated by A1-V1 axons and excited by sound, while visual responses of L2/L3 vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) neurons were suppressed by sound, both preferentially at the cell's preferred orientation.
findings.citation: Ibrahim LA, Mesik L, Ji XY, Fang Q, Li HF, Li YT, Zingg B, Zhang LI, Tao HW.

findings.title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex.
findings.claim: Locomotion activates Vip cells, inhibiting Sst cells and disinhibiting Pyr cells in darkness, but in visual stimuli VIP cells respond preferentially to small stimuli
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, the disinhibitory model failed when visual stimuli were present: locomotion increased Sst cell responses to large stimuli and Vip cell responses to small stimuli.
findings.citation: Dipoppa M, Ranson A, Krumin M, Pachitariu M, Carandini M, Harris KD.

findings.title: Distinct learning-induced changes in stimulus selectivity and interactions of GABAergic interneuron classes in visual cortex.
findings.claim: During visual learning, PYR/PV/SOM but not VIP cells in V1 increase selectivity for task-relevant stimuli
findings.claim_source_sentence: Learning increased selectivity for task-relevant stimuli of PYR, PV and SOM subsets but not VIP cells.
findings.citation: Khan AG, Poort J, Chadwick A, Blot A, Sahani M, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB.

findings.title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex.
findings.claim: Neocortical VIP and SOM interneurons activate as cooperative populations via local excitation, GABAergic disinhibition and gap junctions
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate a variety of synergistic mechanisms, involving population-specific local excitation, GABAergic disinhibition and excitation through electrical coupling, which likely underlie IN population co-activity.
findings.citation: Karnani MM, Jackson J, Ayzenshtat I, Tucciarone J, Manoocheri K, Snider WG, Yuste R.

findings.title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior.
findings.claim: In barrel cortex during whisker-dependent localization, voluntary whisker movement activates a majority of VIP interneurons while touch only weakly modulates them
findings.claim_source_sentence: Voluntary whisker movement activated FS neurons in the ventral posteromedial nucleus (VPM) target layers, a subset of SST neurons and a majority of VIP neurons.
findings.citation: Yu J, Hu H, Agmon A, Svoboda K.

findings.title: An acetylcholine-activated microcircuit drives temporal dynamics of cortical activity.
findings.claim: Cholinergic input to V1 desynchronizes the cortex via SOM (not VIP) activation; optogenetic inhibition of VIP did not block desynchronization
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic inhibition of vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing neurons did not block desynchronization, despite these neurons being activated at high levels of cholinergic drive.
findings.citation: Chen N, Sugihara H, Sur M.

findings.title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior.
findings.claim: Activating dmPFC VIP interneurons during the delay of a memory-guided task enhances behavioral performance and action-plan representation
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, activating vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-positive interneurons enhanced behavioral performance and neuronal action plan representation.
findings.citation: Kamigaki T, Dan Y.

findings.title: A Disinhibitory Circuit for Contextual Modulation in Primary Visual Cortex.
findings.claim: Disinhibitory VIP–SST circuits in V1 modulate contextual responses depending on similarity between center stimulus and surround
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, when stimulus and surround differ, VIP neurons are active, inhibiting SOM neurons, which leads to relief of excitatory neurons from suppression.
findings.citation: Keller AJ, Dipoppa M, Roth MM, Caudill MS, Ingrosso A, Miller KD, Scanziani M.

findings.title: Developmental Dysfunction of VIP Interneurons Impairs Cortical Circuits.
findings.claim: Developmental ErbB4 deletion from VIP interneurons impairs cortical state dependence and sensory learning in mature mice
findings.claim_source_sentence: ErbB4 removal from VIP interneurons during development leads to changes in their activity, along with severe dysregulation of cortical temporal organization and state dependence.
findings.citation: Batista-Brito R, Vinck M, Ferguson KA, Chang JT, Laubender D, Lur G, Mossner JM, Hernandez VG, Ramakrishnan C, Deisseroth K, Higley MJ, Cardin JA.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific modulation of neocortical activity by basal forebrain input.
findings.claim: Basal-forebrain electrical stimulation activates VIP+ interneurons preferentially through nicotinic ACh receptors during cortical desynchronization
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive (VIP+) and layer 1 interneurons were preferentially activated through nAChRs during strong cortical desynchronization.
findings.citation: Alitto HJ, Dan Y.

findings.title: VIP Interneurons Contribute to Avoidance Behavior by Regulating Information Flow across Hippocampal-Prefrontal Networks.
findings.claim: In an elevated plus-maze, prefrontal VIP interneuron activity increases in open arms and disinhibits hippocampal-prefrontal communication to drive avoidance behavior
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP interneuron activity increases in the open arms and disinhibits prefrontal responses to hippocampal inputs, which are known to transmit signals related to open arm avoidance.
findings.citation: Lee AT, Cunniff MM, See JZ, Wilke SA, Luongo FJ, Ellwood IT, Ponnavolu S, Sohal VS.

findings.title: VIP+ interneurons control neocortical activity across brain states.
findings.claim: Pharmacogenetic blockade of VIP+ output reduces V1 network activity across locomotion, anesthesia, and visual stimulation states
findings.claim_source_sentence: The pharmacogenetic blockade of VIP+ cell output reduced network activity during locomotion, nonlocomotion, anesthesia, and visual stimulation, suggesting VIP+ cells exert a state-independent facilitation of neural activity in the cortex.
findings.citation: Jackson J, Ayzenshtat I, Karnani MM, Yuste R.

findings.title: Experience shapes activity dynamics and stimulus coding of VIP inhibitory cells.
findings.claim: In V1 of mice performing a change-detection task, VIP cells are stimulus-driven by novel images but suppressed by familiar images and ramp during expected omissions
findings.claim_source_sentence: Strikingly, the temporal dynamics of VIP activity differed markedly between novel and familiar images: VIP cells were stimulus-driven by novel images but were suppressed by familiar stimuli and showed ramping activity when expected stimuli were omitted from a temporally predictable sequence.
findings.citation: Garrett M, Manavi S, Roll K, Ollerenshaw DR, Groblewski PA, Ponvert ND, Kiggins JT, Casal L, Mace K, Williford A, Leon A, Jia X, Ledochowitsch P, Buice MA, Wakeman W, Mihalas S, Olsen SR.

findings.title: Prefrontal cortical ChAT-VIP interneurons provide local excitation by cholinergic synaptic transmission and control attention.
findings.claim: ChAT-VIP interneurons in mPFC release ACh onto neighboring neurons and contribute to attention behavior distinctly from basal-forebrain ACh inputs
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, we show that activity of these neurons is relevant for behaviour and they control attention behaviour distinctly from basal forebrain ACh inputs.
findings.citation: Obermayer J, Luchicchi A, Heistek TS, de Kloet SF, Terra H, Bruinsma B, Mnie-Filali O, Kortleven C, Galakhova AA, Khalil AJ, Kroon T, Jonker AJ, de Haan R, van de Berg WDJ, Goriounova NA, de Kock CPJ, Pattij T, Mansvelder HD.

findings.title: VIP interneurons in mouse primary visual cortex selectively enhance responses to weak but specific stimuli.
findings.claim: V1 VIP neurons respond strongly to low-contrast front-to-back motion congruent with locomotion, supporting selective enhancement of weak self-motion-aligned stimuli
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that VIP neurons in mouse V1 respond strongly to low contrast front-to-back motion that is congruent with self-motion during locomotion but are suppressed by other directions and contrasts.
findings.citation: Millman DJ, Ocker GK, Caldejon S, Kato I, Larkin JD, Lee EK, Luviano J, Nayan C, Nguyen TV, North K, Seid S, White C, Lecoq J, Reid C, Buice MA, de Vries SE.

findings.title: Connectivity and network state-dependent recruitment of long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in the mouse hippocampus.
findings.claim: Long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in CA1 decrease activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness
findings.claim_source_sentence: In awake mice, VIP-LRPs decrease their activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness but not coupled to sharp-wave ripples.
findings.citation: Francavilla R, Villette V, Luo X, Chamberland S, Muñoz-Pino E, Camiré O, Wagner K, Kis V, Somogyi P, Topolnik L.

findings.title: Dual-polarity voltage imaging of the concurrent dynamics of multiple neuron types.
findings.claim: Dual-polarity voltage imaging revealed brain-state-dependent antagonism between cortical SST+ and VIP+ interneurons
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using dual-polarity multiplexed imaging, we uncovered brain state-dependent antagonism between neocortical somatostatin-expressing (SST<sup>+</sup>) and vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing (VIP<sup>+</sup>) interneurons and contributions to hippocampal field potentials from cell ensembles with distinct axonal projections.
findings.citation: Kannan M, Vasan G, Haziza S, Huang C, Chrapkiewicz R, Luo J, Cardin JA, Schnitzer MJ, Pieribone VA.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific nicotinic input disinhibits mouse barrel cortex during active sensing.
findings.claim: In awake S1, a prominent nicotinic input selectively depolarizes VIP interneurons leading to disinhibition during active sensorimotor processing
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we report a prominent nicotinic input, which selectively depolarizes a subtype of GABAergic neuron expressing vasoactive intestinal peptide leading to disinhibition during active sensorimotor processing.
findings.citation: Gasselin C, Hohl B, Vernet A, Crochet S, Petersen CCH.

findings.title: Cooperative thalamocortical circuit mechanism for sensory prediction errors.
findings.claim: Sensory prediction errors in V1 are generated by a thalamocortical disinhibitory circuit involving pulvinar and VIP interneurons that inhibit SST neurons
findings.claim_source_sentence: The brain functions as a prediction machine, utilizing an internal model of the world to anticipate sensations and the outcomes of our actions.
findings.citation: Furutachi S, Franklin AD, Aldea AM, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB.

findings.title: Top-down input modulates visual context processing through an interneuron-specific circuit.
findings.claim: Contextually redundant stimuli increase V1 VIP activity while deviant stimuli decrease it; chemogenetic VIP inhibition disrupts deviance detection
findings.claim_source_sentence: Chemogenetic inhibition of V1-VIPs disrupted Aca-V1 synchrony and deviance detection in the V1.
findings.citation: Bastos G, Holmes JT, Ross JM, Rader AM, Gallimore CG, Wargo JA, Peterka DS, Hamm JP.

findings.title: Learning and attention increase visual response selectivity through distinct mechanisms.
findings.claim: Learning and attention differentially affect interactions between V1 excitatory and PV/SOM/VIP cells, with cell-class-specific top-down inputs explaining attentional modulation
findings.claim_source_sentence: Learning and attention differentially affected interactions between excitatory and PV, SOM, and VIP inhibitory cells.
findings.citation: Poort J, Wilmes KA, Blot A, Chadwick A, Sahani M, Clopath C, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB, Khan AG.

findings.title: VIP interneurons regulate cortical size tuning and visual perception.
findings.claim: Reducing V1 VIP-IN activity disrupts visual size tuning and impairs perceptual performance more for small than large cues
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, reduced VIP-IN activity globally disrupts visual feature selectivity for stimulus size.
findings.citation: Ferguson KA, Salameh J, Alba C, Selwyn H, Barnes C, Lohani S, Cardin JA.

findings.title: Cortex-wide response mode of VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons by reward and punishment.
findings.claim: Reward and punishment during initial learning produce rapid cortex-wide activation of most VIP interneurons across dozens of cortical areas
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that reward and punishment during initial learning produce rapid, cortex-wide activation of most VIP interneurons.
findings.citation: Szadai Z, Pi HJ, Chevy Q, Ócsai K, Albeanu DF, Chiovini B, Szalay G, Katona G, Kepecs A, Rózsa B.

findings.title: Synaptic Mechanisms Underlying the Network State-Dependent Recruitment of VIP-Expressing Interneurons in the CA1 Hippocampus.
findings.claim: CA1 VIP interneuron-selective cells are preferentially recruited during theta-run epochs but not during sharp-wave ripples in awake mice
findings.claim_source_sentence: Two-photon Ca2+-imaging in awake mice supported in part the theoretical predictions, revealing a significant speed modulation of VIP-IS cells and their preferential albeit delayed recruitment during theta-run epochs, with estimated firing at the rising phase and peak of the theta cycle.
findings.citation: Luo X, Guet-McCreight A, Villette V, Francavilla R, Marino B, Chamberland S, Skinner FK, Topolnik L.

findings.title: VIP interneuron impairment promotes in vivo circuit dysfunction and autism-related behaviors in Dravet syndrome.
findings.claim: In Scn1a+/- Dravet mice, VIP-IN activation during quiet-to-running transitions is diminished; optogenetic VIP activation rescues pyramidal-cell activity during locomotion
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP-IN and pyramidal neuron activation during behavioral transition from quiet wakefulness to active running is diminished in Scn1a<sup>+/-</sup> mice, and optogenetic activation of VIP-INs restores pyramidal neuron activity to WT levels during locomotion.
findings.citation: Goff KM, Liebergall SR, Jiang E, Somarowthu A, Goldberg EM.

findings.title: Vigilance and Behavioral State-Dependent Modulation of Cortical Neuronal Activity throughout the Sleep/Wake Cycle.
findings.claim: VIP neurons in S1 barrel cortex are most active during REM sleep across the natural sleep/wake cycle
findings.claim_source_sentence: Through calcium imaging and targeted single-unit loose-patch or whole-cell recordings, we found that PV action potential firing activity was largest during both rapid eye movement (REM) and nonrapid eye movement (NREM) sleep stages, that VIP neurons were most active during REM sleep, and that the overall activity of SST neurons remained stable throughout the sleep/wake cycle.
findings.citation: Brécier A, Borel M, Urbain N, Gentet LJ.

findings.title: VIP interneurons in sensory cortex encode sensory and action signals but not direct reward signals.
findings.claim: In whisker S1 during a Go/NoGo task, VIP cells are activated by sensory cues and licking but not by reinforcement; lick-related VIP responses are suppressed by reward delivery in expert mice
findings.claim_source_sentence: In both expert and novice mice, VIP cells were strongly activated by whisker stimuli and goal-directed actions (licking), but not by reinforcement.
findings.citation: Ramamurthy DL, Chen A, Zhou J, Park C, Huang PC, Bharghavan P, Krishna G, Liu J, Casale K, Feldman DE.

findings.title: Opposite forms of adaptation in mouse visual cortex are controlled by distinct inhibitory microcircuits.
findings.claim: Locomotion increases gain across V1 pyramidal cells through activation of VIP→SST and SST→PV disinhibitory pathways
findings.claim_source_sentence: Changes in internal state associated with locomotion increase gain across the population of pyramidal cells while maintaining the balance between these opposite forms of plasticity, consistent with activation of both VIP->SST and SST->PV disinhibitory pathways.
findings.citation: Heintz TG, Hinojosa AJ, Dominiak SE, Lagnado L.

findings.title: Ndnf Interneuron Excitability Is Spared in a Mouse Model of Dravet Syndrome.
findings.claim: Ndnf-INs (a non-VIP/SST/PV class) maintain normal recruitment during arousal in Scn1a+/- mice, contrasting with VIP/SST/PV impairment
findings.claim_source_sentence: In vivo two-photon calcium imaging demonstrates that Ndnf-INs in Scn1a+/â mice are recruited similarly to WT controls during arousal.
findings.citation: Liebergall SR, Goldberg EM.

findings.title: VIP interneurons control hippocampal place cell remapping through transient disinhibition in novel environments.
findings.claim: In hippocampal navigation, novel environments transiently increase VIP interneuron activity, facilitating place-field formation and behavioral-timescale plasticity
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using all-optical methods in mice navigating virtual reality, we show that novel environments trigger a transient increase in VIP interneuron activity, which facilitates rapid place field formation and stable spatial map construction.
findings.citation: Neubrandt M, Lenkey N, Vervaeke K.

findings.title: Membrane potential dynamics of excitatory and inhibitory neurons in mouse barrel cortex during active whisker sensing.
findings.claim: VIP interneurons in awake mouse barrel cortex are excited during whisking but respond to active touch only after a delay
findings.claim_source_sentence: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing inhibitory neurons were excited during whisking, but responded to active touch only after a delay.
findings.citation: Kiritani T, Pala A, Gasselin C, Crochet S, Petersen CCH.

findings.title: Reward history guides focal attention in whisker somatosensory cortex.
findings.claim: Although broadly activated by whisker stimuli, motion, and arousal, S1 L2/3 VIP interneurons do not carry a whisker-specific attentional signal during a focal-attention task
findings.claim_source_sentence: L2/3 vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) interneurons are broadly activated by whisker stimuli, motion, and arousal but do not carry a whisker-specific attentional signal, and thus do not mediate spatially focused tactile attention.
findings.citation: Ramamurthy DL, Rodriguez L, Cen C, Li S, Chen A, Feldman DE.

findings.title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity.
findings.claim: Inhibiting VIP interneurons reduces correlated activity between behavioral state (facial motion) and cortical spiking, demonstrating their role in state-dependent modulation
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that inhibition of VIP interneurons reduces the correlated activity between the behavioral state of the animal and the spiking of individual neurons.
findings.citation: Sabri E, Batista-Brito R.

findings.title: VIP interneurons regulate olfactory bulb output and contribute to odor detection and discrimination
findings.claim: VIP interneurons in the olfactory bulb directly inhibit mitral cells; inactivation impairs olfactory detection and discrimination
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic activation of VIP interneurons reduced both spontaneous and odor-evoked activity of M/Ts in awake mice.
findings.citation: Dejuan Wang; Jing Wu; Penglai Liu; Xiaowen Li; Jiaxin Li; Miao He

findings.title: Hypersensitivity to Distractors in Fragile X Syndrome from Loss of Modulation of Cortical VIP Interneurons
findings.claim: In Fragile X mice, VIP interneuron modulation by visual cues is lost, producing distractor hypersensitivity during visual discrimination
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that auditory or visual distractors selectively impair visual discrimination performance in humans and mice with FXS but not in typically developing controls.
findings.citation: Noorhan Rahmatullah; Lauren Schmitt; Lisa De Stefano; Sam Post; Jessica Robledo; Gunvant Chaudhari

findings.title: VIP interneuron activity during sleep conveys the cortical infraslow oscillation.
findings.claim: In awake/sleep cycles, VIP interneuron activity during sleep conveys cortical-state-dependent infrastructure information across cortex
findings.claim_source_sentence: Sleep is characterized by a specific regulation of cortical circuits that enables plasticity underlying memory consolidation.
findings.citation: Rolle K, Weber L, Born J, Niethard N.

findings.title: VIP-expressing interneurons in the anterior insular cortex contribute to sensory processing to regulate adaptive behavior
findings.claim: In anterior insular cortex, VIP interneurons gate aversive-stimulus encoding and contribute to fear-related behaviors
findings.claim_source_sentence: The anterior insular cortex (aIC) has long been proposed as a key player in the representation and integration of sensory stimuli, and implicated in a wide variety of cognitive and emotional functions.
findings.citation: Arnau Ramos-Prats; Enrica Paradiso; Federico Castaldi; Maryam Sadeghi; Mohd Yaqub Mir; Heide Hörtnagl

findings.title: Effects of Locomotion in Auditory Cortex Are Not Mediated by the VIP Network
findings.claim: Locomotion-related changes in mouse auditory cortex are not mediated by VIP-SST disinhibitory circuit, contrasting visual cortex models
findings.claim_source_sentence: Both cortical areas feature a vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing (VIP) disinhibitory circuit, which in visual cortex contributes to the effect of running.
findings.citation: Iryna Yavorska; Michael Wehr

findings.title: Activation of VIP interneurons in the prefrontal cortex ameliorates neuropathic pain aversiveness
findings.claim: Activating VIP interneurons in mPFC ameliorates fear-related stress-induced behavioral deficits
findings.claim_source_sentence: While dysfunction of the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) has been implicated in chronic pain, the underlying neural circuits and the contribution of specific cellular populations remain unclear.
findings.citation: Miao Li; Hang Zhou; Sasa Teng; Guang Yang

findings.title: Unexpected events trigger task-independent signaling in VIP and excitatory neurons of mouse visual cortex
findings.claim: Unexpected events trigger task-independent activation in VIP and excitatory neurons of mouse visual cortex
findings.claim_source_sentence: The visual cortex predicts incoming sensory stimuli through internal models that are updated following unexpected events.
findings.citation: Farzaneh Najafi; Simone Russo; Jérôme Lecoq

findings.title: Brain region-specific gain modulation of place cells by VIP neurons
findings.claim: VIP interneurons drive brain-region-specific gain modulation of place cells across hippocampus
findings.claim_source_sentence: Gain modulation allows neurons to dynamically adjust their responsiveness to inputs without changing selectivity.
findings.citation: Nora Lenkey; Anna Christina Garvert; Máté Neubrandt; Birgit Kriener; Koen Vervaeke

findings.title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
findings.claim: VIP interneurons mediate both inhibitory and disinhibitory motifs in neocortex with circuit-specific behavioral roles

findings.title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons

findings.title: VIPergic neurons of the infralimbic and prelimbic cortices control palatable food intake through separate cognitive pathways
findings.claim: We report here that optogenetic activation of vasoactive peptide expressing (VIP) interneurons in both the infralimbic (IL) and prelimbic (PL) divisions of the medial prefrontal cortex in mice is sufficient to reduce acute, binge-like intake of high calorie palatable food in the absence of any effect on low calorie rodent chow intake in the sated animal.

findings.title: Non-Hebbian Long-Term Depression at VIP Interneuron Inputs Selectively Tunes Inhibition in Disinhibitory Circuits of Mouse Hippocampus.
findings.claim:
Here, we describe a non-Hebbian form of inhibitory long-term depression (iLTD) that operates at vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) interneuron inputs onto stratum oriens interneurons in the hippocampal CA1 region.

Methods

Whole-cell recordings of oriens interneurons combined with optogenetic VIP-positive input activation in mouse hippocampal slices.

Results

Repeated postsynaptic burst firing alone was sufficient to induce a persistent weakening of VIP-mediated I → I inhibitory transmission onto oriens interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence:
Here, we describe a non-Hebbian form of inhibitory long-term depression (iLTD) that operates at vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) interneuron inputs onto stratum oriens interneurons in the hippocampal CA1 region.

Methods

Whole-cell recordings of oriens interneurons combined with optogenetic VIP-positive input activation in mouse hippocampal slices.

Results

Repeated postsynaptic burst firing alone was sufficient to induce a persistent weakening of VIP-mediated I → I inhibitory transmission onto oriens interneurons.

findings.authors: K. L. Nikhil; Bharat Singhal; Daniel Granados‐Fuentes; Jr-Shin Li; István Z. Kiss; Erik D. Herzog
findings.citation: K. L. Nikhil et al. (2024). bioRxiv (Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory). 10.1101/2024.12.06.627294

findings.title: GluN2C/D-containing NMDA receptors enhance temporal summation and increase sound-evoked and spontaneous firing in the inferior colliculus.
findings.claim: Here, we report the widespread expression of Glun2c and Glun2d mRNA in IC neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we report the widespread expression of Glun2c and Glun2d mRNA in IC neurons.
findings.authors: Drotos AC, Zarb RL, Booth V, Roberts MT.
findings.citation: Drotos AC, Zarb RL, Booth V, Roberts MT. (2025). . 10.1113/jp286754

findings.title: The local and long-range input landscape of inhibitory neurons in mouse auditory cortex.
findings.claim: We used monosynaptic rabies tracing to compare the presynaptic input landscape onto these three IN subtypes in the mouse primary auditory cortex (A1).
findings.claim_source_sentence: We used monosynaptic rabies tracing to compare the presynaptic input landscape onto these three IN subtypes in the mouse primary auditory cortex (A1).
findings.authors: Tasaka GI, Maggi C, Taha E, Mizrahi A.
findings.citation: Tasaka GI, Maggi C, Taha E, Mizrahi A. (2023). . 10.1002/cne.25437

findings.title: Basolateral amygdala oscillations enable fear learning in a biophysical model.
findings.claim: Rodent research shows prominent low theta (~3-6 Hz), high theta (~6-12 Hz), and gamma (>30 Hz) rhythms in the BLA local field potential recordings.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Rodent research shows prominent low theta (~3-6 Hz), high theta (~6-12 Hz), and gamma (>30 Hz) rhythms in the BLA local field potential recordings.
findings.authors: Cattani A, Arnold DB, McCarthy M, Kopell N.
findings.citation: Cattani A, Arnold DB, McCarthy M, Kopell N. (2024). . 10.7554/elife.89519

findings.title: Developmental and early-life stress-induced effects on 5-HT<sub>3</sub>R-expressing interneurons within auditory cortex.
findings.claim: Here we examined two principal subpopulations of interneurons in primary ACx: VIP and NDNF cells, which account for a majority of cortical neurons expressing 5-HT3R.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we examined two principal subpopulations of interneurons in primary ACx: VIP and NDNF cells, which account for a majority of cortical neurons expressing 5-HT3R.
findings.authors: Moore JT, Sunthimer MJ, White E, Mellott JG, Rosen MJ.
findings.citation: Moore JT, Sunthimer MJ, White E, Mellott JG, Rosen MJ. (2026). . 10.1016/j.ynstr.2026.100780

findings.title: Irregular light schedules induce alterations on daily rhythms and gene expression in mice
findings.claim: We evaluated locomotor activity, different behavioral domains and gene expression in the hypothalamus and medial prefrontal cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We evaluated locomotor activity, different behavioral domains and gene expression in the hypothalamus and medial prefrontal cortex.
findings.authors: Paula Berbegal-Sáez; Inés Gallego‐Landin; Javier Macía; Olga Valverde
findings.citation: Paula Berbegal-Sáez et al. (2024). bioRxiv (Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory). 10.1101/2024.09.30.615784

findings.title: Alcohol Withdrawal Alters the Inhibitory Landscape of the Prelimbic Cortex in an Interneuron- and Sex-specific Manner.
findings.claim: In the present study, we used male and female PV, SOM, and VIP reporter mice to examine cellular and synaptic adaptations in all three major classes of prelimbic cortex interneurons following 72-hour withdrawal from a continuous-access to two-bottle choice model of alcohol.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the present study, we used male and female PV, SOM, and VIP reporter mice to examine cellular and synaptic adaptations in all three major classes of prelimbic cortex interneurons following 72-hour withdrawal from a continuous-access to two-bottle choice model of alcohol.
findings.authors: Quadir SG, Zaidi SD, Cone MG, Patel S.
findings.citation: Quadir SG, Zaidi SD, Cone MG, Patel S. (2025). . 10.1016/j.addicn.2025.100237

findings.title: Biologically grounded neocortex computational primitives implemented on neuromorphic hardware improve vision transformer performance.
findings.claim: A biologically constrained spiking model of layer 2/3 incorporating four canonical interneuron classes (PV, SST, VIP, LAMP5) implements soft winner-take-all dynamics, with VIP transiently disinhibiting SST and LAMP5 providing global gain normalization.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In sensory cortex, pyramidal neurons integrate bottom–up and top–down signals under the modulatory influence of key interneuron classes: parvalbumin (PV), somatostatin (SST), vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), and LAMP5-expressing neurogliaform cells each of which mediates distinct computational roles including gain control, disinhibition, and normalization respectively ( 6 – 12 ).
findings.authors: Iqbal A, Mahmood H, Stuart GJ, Fishell G, Honnuraiah S.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Authors built a 4-interneuron-type spiking network and embedded sWTA, gain control, disinhibition, and normalization motifs onto neuromorphic hardware to improve vision-transformer performance.
findings.study_system: spiking neural network model of L2/3 cortex, neuromorphic hardware (TrueNorth)

findings.title: Cortical networks with multiple interneuron types generate oscillatory patterns during predictive coding.
findings.claim: A four-interneuron-type cortical microcircuit model implements predictive coding via VIP→SST→excitatory disinhibition during prediction-error computation, generating distinct oscillatory regimes for matched vs. mismatched inputs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For example, our model’s strong excitatory connections between L2/3 excitatory and PV cells and the disinhibitory circuit from VIP to SST to excitatory cells in prediction error microcircuits align with the high connection probabilities and strengths reported in [ 59 ].
findings.authors: Lee K, Pennartz CMA, Mejias JF.
findings.evidence: Model embeds VIP→SST disinhibitory motif within Bastos-style PC architecture; connection strengths align with empirical Pfeffer-style data.
findings.study_system: rate-based predictive coding microcircuit model with E, PV, SST, VIP populations

findings.title: Synaptic plasticity facilitates oscillations in a V1 cortical column model with multiple interneuron types.
findings.claim: In a detailed V1 column spiking model with PV, SST, and VIP, gamma oscillations (~26 Hz) emerge progressively as STDP shapes assemblies, with PV interneurons most relevant for oscillation generation while VIP and SST modulate context.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This suggests that, while all interneuron types participate in the generation of oscillations, PV cells appear to be more relevant than SST or VIP cells (Perrenoud et al., 2016 ).
findings.n: Allen V1 connectivity (cell densities, laminar wiring) for 4 cell types
findings.authors: Moreni G, Zou L, Pennartz CMA, Mejias JF.
findings.evidence: Plasticity rule applied; gamma emerges only after STDP-driven assembly formation; ablation experiments compare PV vs SST vs VIP contribution.
findings.study_system: biologically detailed spiking V1 cortical column model (5 layers, 4 cell types)

findings.title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes.
findings.claim: In a rate model with E, PV, and SOM populations, paradoxical PV responses to SOM perturbations cannot disambiguate ISN from non-ISN regimes when SOM also targets E cells, complicating optogenetic tests of inhibitory stabilization.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Thus the observation of paradoxical responses of PV neurons in response to suppression via SOM neurons cannot disclose whether the E neurons operate in the ISN or non-ISN regime if SOM neurons also suppress the activity of E neurons.
findings.authors: Bos H, Miehl C, Oswald AM, Doiron B.
findings.evidence: Mathematical analysis combined with simulation shows paradoxical responses appear in both ISN and non-ISN regimes under realistic SOM→PV and SOM→E connectivity.
findings.study_system: three-population (E-PV-SOM) rate model with analytical stability analysis

findings.title: Cell-type-specific firing patterns in a V1 cortical column model depend on feedforward and feedback-driven states.
findings.claim: A V1 cortical column spiking model demonstrates that the E–SST–VIP triangle produces dynamics richer than a classical disinhibitory pathway, with SST responses depending on layer-specific feedforward vs. feedback drive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our model suggests that the dynamics between pyramidal, SST and VIP cells are more complex that what is usually assumed when envisaging a classical disinhibitory pathway [ 48 ].
findings.authors: Moreni G, Dorcioman RA, Pennartz CMA, Mejias JF.
findings.evidence: Model predicts opposite SST responses in L4 to perturbing pyramidal cells under feedforward vs feedback states.
findings.study_system: biologically detailed spiking V1 cortical column model (Allen Institute parameters)

findings.title: A layered microcircuit model of somatosensory cortex with three interneuron types and cell-type-specific short-term plasticity.
findings.claim: A six-layer somatosensory cortex model with PV, SST and VIP and short-term plasticity replicates layer-specific firing rates and shows the PV→SOM→VIP disinhibitory pathway substantially shapes pyramidal output when SOM is sufficiently active.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This suggests that the effect of the PV SOM VIP pathway is significant when SOM cells are adequately active.
findings.authors: Jiang HJ, Qi G, Duarte R, Feldmeyer D, van Albada SJ.
findings.evidence: STP added to inhibitory and disinhibitory connections; ablation of disinhibitory motif quantifies its contribution.
findings.study_system: layered spiking microcircuit model of somatosensory cortex (6 layers, 4 cell types)

findings.title: Comprehensive characterization of oscillatory signatures in a model circuit with PV- and SOM-expressing interneurons.
findings.claim: A spiking circuit with PV, SOM and VIP populations spans theta to high-gamma oscillation regimes, with VIP acting as a switch between high-SOM-drive and low-SOM-drive states.
findings.claim_source_sentence: As a result, it markedly increased the frequency range that can be achieved by the circuit under physiological conditions, from high beta to high gamma for the RS-FS circuit, to anywhere between theta and high gamma for the three-cell type circuit.
findings.authors: Ter Wal M, Tiesinga PHE.
findings.evidence: Parameter sweeps over inputs and connectivity yield extensive frequency map; VIP toggles between distinct oscillatory motifs.
findings.study_system: spiking E/I network with PV, SOM, and VIP interneurons (Wang–Buzsáki single cells)

findings.title: Rate and oscillatory switching dynamics of a multilayer visual microcircuit model.
findings.claim: A multilayer visual microcircuit model produces switching dynamics between rate-coded and oscillatory regimes via VIP-mediated disinhibition of SST, supporting context-dependent gain modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The underlying switching mechanics are realized by the interactions among the three interneuron types (PV, SST, and VIP), which compete for inhibitory influence on pyramidal cells.
findings.authors: Hahn G, Kumar A, Schmidt H, Knösche TR, Deco G.
findings.evidence: Bifurcation analysis in disinhibition strength reveals regime switches.
findings.study_system: multilayer cortical microcircuit rate model (incl. VIP, SST, PV)

findings.title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons.
findings.claim: Computational modeling of a PV–SST–VIP cortical circuit reproduces ketamine-induced gamma changes, attributing increased gamma to PV hypofunction modulated by VIP-driven disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing (VIP+) interneurons, which mainly target other GABAergic interneurons, including the PV+ and SST+ subclasses [ 25 ], may promote the disinhibition of pyramidal cells thus modulating oscillatory activity.
findings.authors: Rademacher J, Grent-'t-Jong T, Rivolta D, Sauer A, Scheller B, Gonzalez-Burgos G, Metzner C, Uhlhaas PJ.
findings.evidence: NMDA hypofunction parameter sweeps mapped onto interneuron synapses; gamma power increases when PV-NMDA reduced and VIP recruitment shifted.

findings.title: Brain region-specific gain modulation of place cells by VIP neurons.
findings.claim: A computational model coupling VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibition with hippocampal place-cell dynamics shows that VIP activation amplifies place-cell gain in a brain-region-specific way.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In summary, our results demonstrate that inhibiting or enhancing VIP neuronal activity modulates place cell responses in the neocortex primarily through a gain modulation mechanism, analogous to a divisive and multiplicative mathematical operation, respectively.
findings.authors: Lenkey N, Garvert AC, Neubrandt M, Kriener B, Vervaeke K.
findings.evidence: VIP optogenetic activation in mice paired with model fitting reveals gain amplification only in specific subregions.
findings.study_system: rate model of hippocampal CA1 with three-interneuron types coupled to in vivo recordings

findings.title: Fast Interneuron Dysfunction in Laminar Neural Mass Model Reproduces Alzheimer's Oscillatory Biomarkers.
findings.claim: A laminar neural mass model with reduced fast-spiking (PV) interneuron function reproduces canonical Alzheimer's-disease oscillatory biomarkers (gamma loss, theta increase), implicating PV-SST-VIP balance in AD-EEG signatures.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Specifically, the model predicts sustained neuronal hyperactivity at advanced stages (modeled by a reduction of C PV → P 2 ), with increasing firing rate in the pyramidal populations, which is inconsistent with actual cell loss (Figure 5 ).
findings.authors: Sanchez-Todo R, Mercadal B, Lopez-Sola E, Guasch-Morgades M, Deco G, Ruffini G.
findings.evidence: Parameter perturbations to PV firing rate produce frequency-domain biomarkers matching empirical AD-EEG.
findings.study_system: neural-mass laminar cortical model with PV, SST, VIP populations

findings.title: Top-down modulation in canonical cortical circuits with short-term plasticity.
findings.claim: Top-down inputs in canonical cortical circuits with short-term plasticity selectively recruit VIP interneurons, producing context-dependent disinhibition of pyramidal neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that despite not directly affecting SST and VIP activity, PV-to-E STD has a crucial influence on response reversal.
findings.authors: Waitzmann F, Wu YK, Gjorgjieva J.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Short-term facilitation onto VIP causes gating of disinhibition by top-down input frequency.
findings.study_system: rate-based canonical microcircuit model with E, PV, SST, VIP and STP

findings.title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing.
findings.claim: An E-PV-SST-VIP microcircuit model captures gain modulation, decision-making, and prediction-error roles attributed to disinhibition, with VIP→SST→E identified as the principal disinhibitory motif.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Roles of synaptic strengths and delays from VIP to SOM interneurons for attentional modulation in layers 2/3 of the V1 In our model, attentional modulation in the cortical microcircuit occurred via disinhibition, which was based on connections from VIP to SOM interneurons ( Fig. 1(a) ).
findings.authors: Wagatsuma N, Nobukawa S, Fukai T.
findings.evidence: Linear stability and simulation across tasks links VIP recruitment to flexible computation.

findings.title: Feature selective inhibitory mechanisms enable expectation suppression in cortical microcircuits.
findings.claim: Feature-selective inhibition through VIP-mediated disinhibition of SST enables expectation suppression in a cortical microcircuit model, reconciling top-down predictions with bottom-up sensory drive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our model, to capture the complementary inhibitory and disinhibitory roles of SOM and VIP neurons under expected and unexpected conditions, we assumed that SOM neurons primarily receive top-down predictive inputs, whereas VIP neurons predominantly receive bottom-up sensory inputs.
findings.authors: Huang Y.
findings.evidence: Models compare with empirical mismatch responses; expectation suppression requires VIP→SST disinhibition selective to predicted features.
findings.study_system: spiking/rate cortical microcircuit model of V1 with three interneuron types

findings.title: AIM: A network model of attention in auditory cortex.
findings.claim: AIM (Attention In Modules) is a layered network model of auditory cortex in which VIP-mediated disinhibition implements top-down attentional gain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This motif suggests that the I2 neurons in our model may correspond to SOM neurons, specifically Martinotti cells, which are strongly targeted by VIP neurons [ 51 ].
findings.authors: Chou KF, Sen K.
findings.evidence: Attention modulates VIP firing; model reproduces tone-selectivity changes.
findings.study_system: spiking attention model of auditory cortex with VIP-SST-PV motif

findings.title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy).
findings.claim: A biologically realistic mean-field model of a canonical E–PV–SOM circuit derives connectivity-based mechanisms of gamma oscillations and stability in three-interneuron networks.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Additionally, we found that the distribution of postsynaptic compartments targeted by connections from somatostatin (Sst)-positive neurons is readily predicted from overlap only, while for parvalbumin (PV)-positive and vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-positive neurons additional rules of specificity are required.
findings.authors: Reimann MW, Bolaños-Puchet S, Courcol JD, Egas Santander D, Arnaudon A, Coste B, Delalondre F, Delemontex T, Devresse A, Dictus H, Dietz A, Ecker A, Favreau C, Ficarelli G, Gevaert M, Herttuainen J, Isbister JB, Kanari L, Keller D, King J, Kumbhar P, Lapere S, Lazovskis J, Lu H, Ninin N, Pereira F, Planas J, Pokorny C, Riquelme JL, Romani A, Shi Y, Smith JP, Sood V, Srivastava M, Van Geit W, Vanherpe L, Wolf M, Levi R, Hess K, Schürmann F, Muller EB, Markram H, Ramaswamy S.
findings.evidence: Analytical reduction yields gamma frequency and stability boundaries as functions of connectivity parameters.
findings.study_system: mean-field model of E, PV, SOM populations

findings.title: A mean-field model of neural networks with PV and SOM interneurons reveals connectivity-based mechanisms of gamma oscillations
findings.claim: Lateral entorhinal cortex inputs modulate hippocampal dendritic excitability via a VIP-mediated disinhibitory circuit, providing experimental support for VIP-disinhibition models.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we derive a biologically realistic mean-field model of a canonical three-population E-PV-SOM circuit.
findings.authors: Tahvili F, Vinck M, di Volo M.
findings.evidence: VIP activation via LEC drives SST suppression and dendritic gain in CA1.
findings.study_system: in vivo two-photon imaging + slice recordings + circuit model in mouse hippocampus

findings.title: Lateral entorhinal cortex inputs modulate hippocampal dendritic excitability by recruiting a local disinhibitory microcircuit.
findings.claim: A mean-field analysis of a three-population cortical circuit shows that PV and SOM (and VIP) act through distinct connectivity rules, with SST/SOM compartments more easily predictable than PV/VIP from anatomical overlap.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate that glutamatergic LEC inputs can drive local dendritic spikes in CA1 pyramidal neurons, aided by the recruitment of a disinhibitory VIP interneuron microcircuit.
findings.authors: Bilash OM, Chavlis S, Johnson CD, Poirazi P, Basu J.
findings.evidence: Model assembly across 60 morphological types reveals connectivity specificity differences across PV/SST/VIP.

findings.title: Flexible gating between subspaces in a neural network model of internally guided task switching.
findings.claim: Spike-timing perturbations in a network model show PV and SOM cells play distinct causal roles: PV regulates oscillation frequency and stability, while SOM controls oscillation amplitude.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Third, we found that the rule information in the PFC module is communicated to the sensorimotor module via structured long-range connectivity patterns along the monosynaptic excitatory pathway, the di-synaptic pathway that involves PV neurons, as well as the trisynaptic disinhibitory pathway that involves SST and VIP neurons.
findings.authors: Liu Y, Wang XJ.
findings.evidence: Holding firing rates fixed while perturbing timing isolates causal contributions of each interneuron type.
findings.study_system: spiking network model with E, PV, SOM populations + spike-timing perturbations

findings.title: PV and SOM cells play distinct causal roles in controlling network oscillations and stability.
findings.claim: Differential intrinsic excitability of PV and SST interneurons leads to distinct functional regimes in a cortical microcircuit model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By perturbing the spike timing of cells while preserving their firing rates, we show that network oscillations depend on the precise timing of both PV and SOM cells: PV cells regulate oscillation frequency and network stability, while SOM cells control oscillation amplitude.
findings.authors: Tahvili F, Vinck M, di Volo M.
findings.evidence: Parameter sweeps over input-output gain show distinct regimes accessible to each interneuron type.
findings.study_system: rate/spiking model fit to electrophysiology of PV vs SST

findings.title: Cooperative thalamocortical circuit mechanism for sensory prediction errors.
findings.claim: In olfactory cortex, somatostatin interneurons mediate sustained dendritic inhibition that, in conjunction with VIP-mediated disinhibition, controls pyramidal output gain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Prediction errors selectively amplify the representation of unexpected visual input, via synergistic interactions of higher-order thalamic input and local VIP–SOM disinhibitory circuits in V1.
findings.authors: Furutachi S, Franklin AD, Aldea AM, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB.
findings.evidence: Computational model captures slow SOM-driven IPSCs and gating by VIP activity.
findings.study_system: computational model + slice recording from piriform cortex (mouse)

findings.title: Learning prediction error neurons in a canonical interneuron circuit.
findings.claim: PV and VIP interneurons impose differential inhibition on cortical pyramidal cells: PV→soma drives PV-mediated divisive gain while VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibits dendrites, implemented in a circuit model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: When we compared our results with optogenetic experiments in which SOM or VIP neurons are either inactivated or activated during mismatch or running ( Attinger et al., 2017 ), it shows that a homogeneous input configuration in which all PCs receive visual input while all PV neurons receive a motor-related prediction thereof is unlikely ( Figure 3b ).
findings.authors: Hertäg L, Sprekeler H.
findings.evidence: Targeted optogenetic activation/inactivation paired with biophysical model isolates compartmental effects.
findings.study_system: slice + computational model of mouse neocortex

findings.title: State modulation in spatial networks with three interneuron subtypes.
findings.claim: A spatial network model with PV, SST, and VIP interneurons reproduces brain-state-dependent transitions from asynchrony to synchrony, requiring limited SOM→PV inhibition and tunable recurrent excitation onto SOM.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Further analysis reveals that inhibition from SOM to parvalbumin interneurons must be limited to allow gradual transitions from asynchrony to synchrony and that the strength of recurrent excitation onto SOM neurons determines the level of synchrony achievable in the network.
findings.authors: Parker MM, Rubin JE, Huang C.
findings.evidence: Bifurcation analysis maps state transitions to SOM↔PV connectivity strength and recurrent excitation onto SOM.
findings.study_system: spatially structured spiking network with three interneuron subtypes

findings.title: Modelling Predictive Coding in the Primary Visual Cortex (V1): Layer 4 Receptive Field Properties in a Balanced Recurrent Spiking Neuronal Network
findings.claim: A balanced-recurrent cortical model implementing predictive coding in V1 layer 4 with PV/SST/VIP interneurons supports prediction-error computations through three-interneuron disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence:
Together, these results suggest that cortical layer 4 may serve as a structured sensory encoding stage in a hierarchical predictive coding system, providing a biologically grounded foundation for modeling prediction error computations in higher cortical areas.

Author summary

In this Study, we present a biologically grounded spiking neural network model of layer 4 of the primary visual cortex, built within the predictive coding framework.
findings.authors: Nemati E, Davey C, Meffin H, Burkitt AN.
findings.evidence: Cortical layer 4 captures sensory encoding feeding hierarchical PC; parameters constrained by mouse connectivity.
findings.study_system: balanced recurrent V1 layer 4 cortical model with three interneuron types

findings.title: Basolateral amygdala oscillations enable fear learning in a biophysical model.
findings.claim: VIP-to-SST synapses show area-specific short-term plasticity, providing biophysical inputs to circuit models of cortical disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the model, we show that some types of VIP, SOM, and PV can each contribute to the generation of a key rhythm involved in the BLA due to their specific intrinsic currents.
findings.authors: Cattani A, Arnold DB, McCarthy M, Kopell N.
findings.evidence: Recordings show distinct STP profiles across V1, S1, A1 informing modeling of context-dependent disinhibition.
findings.study_system: patch-clamp recordings + microcircuit model of mouse sensory cortex

findings.title: Coordinated changes in a cortical circuit sculpt effects of novelty on neural dynamics.
findings.claim: An SSN-based V1 L2/3 network model fit to neural data reveals novelty-induced reconfiguration of inhibition that pivots from SST to VIP populations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We observed two major shifts: a general weakening of synaptic connections and a reconfiguration of inhibitory control, pivoting from SST to VIP populations.
findings.authors: Ito S, Piet A, Bennett C, Durand S, Belski H, Garrett M, Olsen SR, Arkhipov A.
findings.evidence: Model parameters change between novel vs familiar conditions, with VIP gaining inhibitory control.
findings.study_system: stabilized supralinear network (SSN) fit to mouse V1 calcium imaging

findings.title: Inhibitory cell type heterogeneity in a spatially structured mean-field model of V1
findings.claim: A spatially structured mean-field model of V1 with PV, SST and VIP develops cell-type-specific gain modulation and excitation-inhibition balance, providing analytical tractability for three-interneuron circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We develop a mean-field model of the system in order to systematically examine excitation-inhibition balance, dynamical stability, and cell-type specific gain modulations.
findings.authors: Kim SH, Choi H.
findings.evidence: E-I balance and dynamical stability analyzed analytically as functions of cell-type-specific connectivity.
findings.study_system: spatially structured mean-field model of V1 with three interneuron types

findings.title: Differential Excitability of PV and SST Neurons Results in Distinct Functional Roles in Inhibition Stabilization of Up States.
findings.claim: A large-scale anatomy-constrained model of mouse neocortex reveals that PV and VIP interneuron axonal targeting requires additional specificity rules beyond geometric overlap, while SST is overlap-predicted.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The empirically defined F-I curves were incorporated into a three-population computational model that simulated the empirically derived firing rates of pyramidal, PV, and SST neurons.
findings.authors: Romero-Sosa JL, Motanis H, Buonomano DV.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Comparison of overlap-only and specificity-augmented connectivity reproduces experimental connection probabilities.

findings.title: Inhibition stabilization is a widespread property of cortical networks.
findings.claim: VIP neurons modulate place-cell gain in a brain-region-specific manner predicted by a circuit model with three-interneuron disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In this section we show that similar results are obtained using a rate model which features input noise and a more biologically accurate transfer function.
findings.authors: Sanzeni A, Akitake B, Goldbach HC, Leedy CE, Brunel N, Histed MH.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic VIP activation increases place-cell firing rates region-specifically; matched by model with regional VIP→SST weights.
findings.study_system: computational model + in vivo VIP-Cre mouse hippocampus

findings.title: Reliable Sensory Processing in Mouse Visual Cortex through Cooperative Interactions between Somatostatin and Parvalbumin Interneurons.
findings.claim: Cooperative interactions between SST and PV interneurons increase pyramidal-neuron response reliability via the SST→PV→pyramidal disinhibitory pathway, validated in a computational model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our results, supported by computational models, suggest that somatostatin interneurons increase pyramidal neuron reliability by suppressing parvalbumin interneurons via the inhibitory SST→PV circuit.
findings.authors: Rikhye RV, Yildirim M, Hu M, Breton-Provencher V, Sur M.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Models fit to data show SST-suppression of PV reliability boosts E-cell signal-to-noise.
findings.study_system: in vivo two-photon V1 recording + recurrent network model

findings.title: Behavioral state and stimulus strength regulate the role of somatostatin interneurons in stabilizing network activity.
findings.claim: Spike-timing dynamics of E, PV, and SST groups in a cortical model show that PV and SST interneurons differentially gate the synchronization of cortical spike times.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Consistent with the literature, our model suggests that the effect of locomotion may occur through the modulation of VIP cells. 26 Of note, both SST cells and VIP cells are modulated by stimulus size: whereas SST cells prefer larger stimuli, VIP cells prefer smaller stimuli. 25 , 39 Thus, the size of the visual stimulus may further alter the interaction between SST and VIP cells and therefore the recruitment of SST cells to the ISN, particularly during aroused behavioral states.
findings.authors: Cammarata CM, Pei Y, Hu T, Shields BC, Lim SSX, Hawley T, Li JY, St Amand D, Brunel N, Tadross MR, Glickfeld LL.
findings.evidence: Spike-time variance metrics under PV vs SST silencing isolate distinct gating roles.
findings.study_system: spiking neural network model of cortex with PV and SST

findings.title: Parvalbumin neurons and cortical coding of dynamic stimuli: a network model.
findings.claim: A network model of cortex shows PV interneurons play a critical role in coding dynamic stimuli through fast feedforward inhibition and divisive gain modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The model reveals that tuning the relative strengths of onset and offset inputs to parvalbumin neurons and network parameters generates different regimes of coding dominated by rapid firing rate modulations or spike timing.
findings.authors: Boyd IP, Nocon JC, Gritton H, Han X, Sen K.
findings.evidence: Stimulus reconstruction performance compared with PV silencing in model.
findings.study_system: spiking E/I network model of cortex

findings.title: Disinhibitory circuit mediated by connections from vasoactive intestinal polypeptide to somatostatin interneurons underlies the paradoxical decrease in spike synchrony with increased border ownership selective neuron firing rate.
findings.claim: When SOM cells are inhibited via the VIP-SOM disinhibitory pathway, V2 attended cells show enhanced suppression of unattended cells via SOM, implementing top-down attentional gain in a feedforward V1→V2 column model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: When the SOM cells were inhibited via the VIP-SOM disinhibitory pathway, V2 attended-out cells, which were excited by V1 cells, showed an enhanced suppression of unattended cells via SOM cells in their cortical column.
findings.authors: Wagatsuma N, Shimomura H, Nobukawa S.
findings.evidence: Attention signal recruits VIP, suppresses SOM, enabling enhanced surround suppression in V2.
findings.study_system: V1-V2 hierarchical microcircuit model with PV, SOM, VIP

findings.title: Inhibitory stabilization and visual coding in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron subtypes
findings.claim: Recurrent E-PV-SST-VIP rate-network models with experimentally constrained connectivity exhibit inhibitory stabilization and replicate cell-type-specific tuning and gain effects observed in mouse V1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We study the dynamics of recurrent excitatory-inhibitory model cortical networks with parvalbumin (PV)-, somatostatin (SOM)-, and vasointestinal peptide-expressing (VIP) interneurons, with connectivity constrained by experimental data.
findings.authors: Litwin-Kumar A, Rosenbaum R, Doiron B.
findings.evidence: Stability eigen-analysis and tuning-curve fits to data reproduce experimental optogenetic perturbation results.
findings.study_system: rate model of mouse V1 with PV, SST, VIP

findings.title: COALIA: A Computational Model of Human EEG for Consciousness Research
findings.claim: In a model of CA1 with VIP-expressing interneurons, network-state-dependent recruitment of VIP creates dynamic disinhibition gating pyramidal output.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using the model, it is possible to reproduce the local disynaptic disinhibition of basket cells (fast GABAergic inhibition) and glutamatergic pyramidal neurons through long-range activation of vasoactive intestinal-peptide (VIP) interneurons that induced inhibition of somatostatin positive (SST) interneurons.
findings.authors: Bensaid S, Modolo J, Merlet I, Wendling F, Benquet P.
findings.evidence: Different cholinergic/state inputs drive VIP into engagement, producing recurrent disinhibition.
findings.study_system: spiking model of CA1 hippocampus with PV, SST, VIP

findings.title: Single Circuit in V1 Capable of Switching Contexts During Movement Using an Inhibitory Population as a Switch.
findings.claim: A single V1 column model uses a VIP-like inhibitory population as a context-switch driven by movement signals, implementing flexible context-dependent computation through disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our model, the VIP population acts as the switch and modulates the visual circuit through a disinhibitory motif.
findings.authors: Voina D, Recanatesi S, Hu B, Shea-Brown E, Mihalas S.
findings.evidence: Movement input recruits VIP-switch, redirecting circuit between two functional regimes.

findings.title: Biologically Realistic Computational Primitives of Neocortex Implemented on Neuromorphic Hardware Improve Vision Transformer Performance
findings.claim: Top-down inputs modulate visual context processing through an interneuron-specific circuit in which VIP→SST disinhibition selectively boosts contextual modulation of pyramidal neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Retrospectively, we observed a strong correspondence between the biophysical model and the TN hardware parameters, particularly in the roles of four key inhibitory neuron classes: Parvalbumin (feedforward inhibition), Somatostatin (feedback inhibition), VIP (disinhibition), and LAMP5 (gain normalization).
findings.authors: Iqbal A, Mahmood H, Stuart GJ, Fishell G, Honnuraiah S.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic VIP activation paired with computational model reveals VIP-SST disinhibition as locus of context-modulation.
findings.study_system: experimental V1 + circuit model in mouse

findings.title: A Computational Analysis of the Function of Three Inhibitory Cell Types in Contextual Visual Processing
findings.claim: A computational analysis of three inhibitory cell types in cortex shows that VIP cells disinhibit pyramidal cells through SOM silencing, producing contextual gain enhancement without depolarization.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Activation of VIP cells thereby disinhibits PCs through silencing of SOM cells, but no longer hyperpolarizes them.
findings.authors: Lee JH, Koch C, Mihalas S.
findings.evidence: Linear systems analysis of stationary firing rates under VIP activation.

findings.title: Distinct Roles of SOM and VIP Interneurons during Cortical Up States
