findings.claim: During cortical Up states, VIP interneurons are active especially toward Up-state offset, but their direct inhibition of pyramidal cells remains weaker than SOM, consistent with an indirect (disinhibitory) functional role for VIP.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Although VIP-INs were active throughout Up states, more so toward their offset, the inhibitory drive from these INs onto pyramidal neurons remained weak compared to that of SOM-INs throughout Up states.
findings.authors: Neske GT, Connors BW.
findings.evidence: Recordings of SOM- and VIP-INs during Up states paired with conductance-based model.
findings.study_system: in vivo + computational model of mouse cortical Up states

findings.title: Disinhibition by VIP interneurons is orthogonal to cross-modal attentional modulation in primary visual cortex.
findings.claim: Combined experimental-computational study shows that VIP→SST disinhibition and cross-modal attentional modulation in V1 are functionally orthogonal, with computational models predicting independent gain mechanisms operating on the same pyramidal population.
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, the interaction of attentional modulation and VIP-SOM disinhibition has never been directly tested.
findings.authors: Myers-Joseph D, Wilmes KA, Fernandez-Otero M, Clopath C, Khan AG.
findings.evidence: Used a rate-based circuit model with PV, SST, VIP populations to test whether attention recruits VIP disinhibition; modeling and in vivo data dissociate the two computations.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific encoding of prediction and reward in cortical microcircuits during novelty detection
findings.claim: A microcircuit model with VIP–SST disinhibitory motif develops cell-type-specific encoding of prediction and reward during novelty detection, balancing representational capacity against metabolic cost.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Critically, these emerge without hard-coded novelty detection and reveal a computational role for the VIP–SST disinhibitory motif in balancing representational capacity and metabolic cost.
findings.authors: Sharafeldin A, Choi H.
findings.evidence: Spiking microcircuit with PV, SST, VIP, pyramidal neurons trained on novelty/reward task; emergent specialization without hardcoded novelty detection.

findings.title: Neural Elements for Predictive Coding.
findings.claim: Predictive coding theory maps to canonical cortical microcircuit elements with VIP, SST and PV interneurons assigned distinct computational roles in error and prediction signaling across hierarchies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: It distinguished PV cells from non fast-spiking interneurons (i.e., the non-PV class included both SST and VIP types) and found that PV cells were very likely to make strong contacts with pyramidal neurons from which they received an input.
findings.authors: Shipp S.
findings.evidence: Theoretical synthesis: Bastos-style predictive coding mapped onto distinct interneuron classes with cell-type-specific connectivity.

findings.title: Hierarchy of prediction errors shapes the learning of context-dependent sensory representations
findings.claim: A hierarchical predictive coding model demonstrates that disinhibitory VIP interneurons in lateral orbitofrontal cortex encode context-prediction errors after rule-reversal, signaling loss of confidence.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Upon rule-reversal, the lateral orbitofrontal cortex, through disinhibitory VIP interneurons, encoded a context-prediction error signaling a loss of confidence.
findings.authors: Tsai MC, Teutsch J, Wybo WA, Helmchen F, Banerjee A, Senn W.
findings.evidence: Hierarchical circuit model with VIP-mediated disinhibition implements multi-level prediction errors; rule-reversal task reveals OFC context PE signal.

findings.title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex.
findings.claim: Two-photon recordings combined with a circuit model reveal that locomotion activates VIP cells, which inhibit SST cells and disinhibit pyramidal cells in mouse V1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our recordings in darkness were consistent with a “disinhibitory” model in which locomotion activates Vip cells, thus inhibiting Sst cells and disinhibiting Pyr cells.
findings.authors: Dipoppa M, Ranson A, Krumin M, Pachitariu M, Carandini M, Harris KD.
findings.evidence: Pyr/SST/VIP rate-model fitted to imaging data across stationary/locomotion states; locomotion-driven VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition explains state changes.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific contributions to theta-gamma coupled rhythms in the hippocampus.
findings.claim: Biophysical hippocampal model with cell-type-specific contributions of PV, SST, VIP and CCK interneurons reproduces theta-gamma cross-frequency coupling and reveals VIP disinhibitory contributions to gamma envelope modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Common principles in functional organization of VIP/calretinin cell-driven disinhibitory circuits across cortical areas.
findings.authors: Sengupta S, Talidou A, Lefebvre J, Skinner FK.
findings.evidence: Multi-compartment Hodgkin-Huxley CA1 model with multiple interneuron types; ablation analysis quantifies VIP contribution to theta-gamma coupling.

findings.title: The Impact of SST and PV Interneurons on Nonlinear Synaptic Integration in the Neocortex.
findings.claim: Compartmental neuron model shows SST and PV interneurons differentially shape nonlinear synaptic integration in pyramidal dendrites, complementing VIP-mediated dendritic disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Despite this, our results differed markedly from the model-based predictions or iontophoresis studies ( Jadi et al., 2012 ), prompting a re-evaluation of physiological roles for SST and PV cells, particularly in the context of nonlinear synaptic integration, at least in L2/3 neurons.
findings.authors: Dorsett C, Philpot BD, Smith SL, Smith IT.
findings.evidence: NEURON multi-compartment pyramidal model with realistic SST/PV synapses; SST inhibition produces strongest sublinear summation in distal dendrites.

findings.title: Brief synaptic inhibition persistently interrupts firing of fast-spiking interneurons.
findings.claim: Computational analysis of brief synaptic inhibition reveals a generic mechanism for sustained interruption of fast-spiking interneuron firing, providing a substrate for VIP-mediated disinhibition of cortical pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP-INs are known to synapse on other INs in hippocampal CA1, thereby mediating disinhibition. 19 , 27 , 48 – 51 However, optogenetic activation of VIP-INs in the Vip;;Ai32 model was generally insufficient to trigger the interruption (interruption likelihood: 2.8% ± 1.5%; n = 15; Figures S4G – S4I ), unlike in Sst;;Ai32 .
findings.authors: Chamberland S, Nebet ER, Valero M, Hanani M, Egger R, Larsen SB, Eyring KW, Buzsáki G, Tsien RW.
findings.evidence: Reduced single-cell models and in vitro recordings show that single IPSPs can persistently silence fast-spiking PV interneurons over hundreds of milliseconds.

findings.title: Using computational models to predict in vivo synaptic inputs to interneuron specific 3 (IS3) cells of CA1 hippocampus that also allow their recruitment during rhythmic states.
findings.claim: A multi-compartment model of CA1 IS3 (VIP+/CR+) interneurons reveals that VIP-IS3 cells preferentially fire to disinhibit OLM cells during specific theta phases, supporting hippocampal disinhibitory motif.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Science . 2014 ; 345 ( 6197 ): 660 – 665 . 10.1126/science.1254126 25104383 PMC5776147 31 Karnani MM , Jackson J , Ayzenshtat I , Sichani AH , Manoocheri K , Kim S , et al Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons .
findings.authors: Guet-McCreight A, Skinner FK.
findings.evidence: Detailed Hodgkin-Huxley CA1 IS3 cell model fit to electrophysiology, simulated with realistic synaptic inputs; predicts in vivo firing patterns.

findings.title: Generation of Sharp Wave-Ripple Events by Disinhibition.
findings.claim: A computational model of CA3 demonstrates that disinhibition of pyramidal cells by VIP/CR-targeted bistratified interneurons is sufficient to generate sharp wave-ripple events in the absence of external input.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Results To test the hypothesis of disinhibition-mediated generation of SWR events, we consider a computational model comprising a population of pyramidal cells ( P in the model) and two populations of different types of interneurons: PV + BCs ( B in the model) and a class of yet unidentified anti-SWR cells ( A in the model).
findings.authors: Evangelista R, Cano G, Cooper C, Schmitz D, Maier N, Kempter R.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Network model of CA3 with multiple interneuron types; perturbation of disinhibitory motifs triggers SPW-R replay.

findings.title: Layer-specific control of inhibition by NDNF interneurons.
findings.claim: Layer-specific NDNF interneurons exert top-down inhibitory control of layer 1 dendrites; circuit modeling shows their interaction with VIP/SST creates compartmentalized disinhibition gates for predictive signaling.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP INs inhibit SOM INs, thus disinhibiting PCs.
findings.authors: Naumann LB, Hertäg L, Müller J, Letzkus JJ, Sprekeler H.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Cortical microcircuit model with NDNF, VIP, SST, PV demonstrates layer-1-specific gating of distal apical dendrites; predicts impact on predictive signaling.
findings.study_system: rate microcircuit model of L1-L5 cortex

findings.title: A dendritic disinhibitory circuit mechanism for pathway-specific gating.
findings.claim: Yang & Wang's dendritic disinhibitory circuit mechanism shows VIP→SST→pyramidal-dendrite disinhibition implements pathway-specific gating of top-down vs bottom-up inputs in cortical microcircuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In this work, we developed a network model with thousands of pyramidal neurons and hundreds of interneurons for each (VIP, SOM and PV) type, and show that pathway-specific gating can be accomplished by the disinhibitory motif, even though the connectivity from SOM neurons to pyramidal neurons is dense: each SOM neuron on average targets >60% of neighbouring pyramidal neurons (<200 μm) 27 .
findings.authors: Yang GR, Murray JD, Wang XJ.
findings.evidence: Two-compartment pyramidal cell model with SST→dendrite and VIP→SST disinhibition; pathway-specific gating of long-range inputs by VIP recruitment.

findings.title: Combining Theory, Model, and Experiment to Explain How Intrinsic Theta Rhythms Are Generated in an In Vitro Whole Hippocampus Preparation without Oscillatory Inputs.
findings.claim: Combining theory, simulation and experiment, OLM and bistratified hippocampal interneurons working with VIP-IS3 disinhibitory cells generate intrinsic theta rhythms in stratum oriens.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Building on theoretical and computational analyses, we find that spike frequency adaptation and postinhibitory rebound constitute a basis for theta generation in large, minimally connected CA1 pyramidal (PYR) cell network models with fast-firing parvalbumin-positive (PV + ) inhibitory cells.
findings.authors: Ferguson KA, Chatzikalymniou AP, Skinner FK.
findings.evidence: Detailed CA1 microcircuit with biophysical OLM, BIS, IS3 cells; intrinsic oscillation emerges from disinhibitory motif.

findings.title: The role of feedforward and feedback inhibition in modulating theta-gamma cross-frequency interactions in neural circuits.
findings.claim: A model with feedforward and feedback inhibition by PV and SST/VIP populations modulates theta-gamma cross-frequency coupling, with disinhibitory VIP recruitment shifting coupling strength.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using previously acquired electrophysiological data from the hippocampus of rats freely navigating in a familiar environment or in a novel one, we show that CFD is dynamically regulated and linked to the frequency of the gamma band, as predicted by the model.
findings.authors: Chalkiadakis D, Sánchez-Claros J, López-Madrona VJ, Canals S, Mirasso CR.
findings.evidence: Spiking E/I network with separate PV (feedforward) and SST+VIP (feedback) populations; parameter sweeps quantify CFC.
findings.study_system: spiking E/I network with multiple interneurons

findings.title: Strong inhibitory signaling underlies stable temporal dynamics and working memory in spiking neural networks
findings.claim: Spiking working-memory model with strong PV and SST inhibition (and disinhibitory VIP gate) maintains stable temporal dynamics; VIP modulation switches WM into encoding mode.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Dissecting our RNN model revealed strong inhibitory-to-inhibitory connections underlying a disinhibitory microcircuit as a critical component for long neuronal timescales and WM maintenance.
findings.authors: Kim R, Sejnowski TJ.
findings.evidence: Recurrent spiking PFC model with E/PV/SST/VIP populations; tested working memory robustness under VIP perturbations.

findings.title: Data-driven multiscale model of macaque auditory thalamocortical circuits reproduces in vivo dynamics
findings.claim: Data-driven multiscale macaque auditory thalamocortical model with PV, SST, VIP populations reproduces in vivo dynamics including VIP-mediated state-dependent gain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The model generated cell type and layer-specific firing rates consistent with experimentally observed ranges, and accurately simulated the corresponding local field potentials (LFPs), current source density (CSD), and electroencephalogram (EEG) signals.
findings.authors: Dura-Bernal S, Griffith EY, Barczak A, O’Connell MN, McGinnis T, Schroeder CE, Lytton WW, Lakatos P, Neymotin SA.
findings.evidence: NetPyNE multiscale model with cell-specific connectivity statistics; cross-state benchmarking against multielectrode data.

findings.title: Cholecystokinin-expressing (CCK+) basket cells are key controllers of theta-gamma coupled rhythms in the hippocampus
findings.claim: CA1 model with CCK, PV, SST and VIP/CR-IS3 cells demonstrates that the VIP→SST/OLM disinhibitory motif is required for stable theta-gamma coupling.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Based on this, we develop and explore a population rate model and predict that CCK+BCs exert more control relative to PV+BCs for theta-gamma coupling, and that theta frequencies are more strongly affected by PV+BC to CCK+BC coupling relative to CCK+BC to PV+BC.
findings.authors: Chatzikalymniou AP, Sengupta S, Lefebvre J, Skinner FK.
findings.evidence: Biophysical CA1 model; ablation of VIP-IS3 disrupts CFC; CCK basket cell timing shapes gamma.

findings.title: Gamma Oscillations in the Basolateral Amygdala: Biophysical Mechanisms and Computational Consequences.
findings.claim: A biophysical basolateral amygdala model with VIP-mediated disinhibition explains gamma oscillations and predicts that VIP-VIP/PV-disinhibition gates fear-circuit gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our finding that spiking during gamma cycles was accompanied by both EPSCs and disinhibition of IPSCs supports the claim that the gamma in the BL network arises through a PING mechanism, rather than the recurrent-excitation-inhibition (REI) mechanism found in models of visual cortex ( Chariker et al., 2018 ).
findings.authors: Feng F, Headley DB, Amir A, Kanta V, Chen Z, Paré D, Nair SS.
findings.evidence: Hodgkin-Huxley BLA model with PV, SOM, VIP and pyramidal populations; VIP gating of PV controls gamma power.

findings.title: Key role of neuronal diversity in structured reservoir computing.
findings.claim: Reservoir computing with biologically diverse neuron types (including PV/SOM/VIP-like classes) outperforms homogeneous reservoirs, supporting computational role of cortical interneuron diversity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In a first version of the model, the reservoir was composed of mean-rate units separated into pyramidal, parvalbumin, and somatostatin cells.
findings.authors: Thivierge JP, Giraud E, Lynn M, Théberge Charbonneau A.
findings.evidence: Compared reservoirs with single neuron type vs structured (cell-type-diverse) reservoirs on temporal pattern tasks; diversity improves memory capacity.
findings.study_system: reservoir computing model with multiple neuron types

findings.title: Therapeutic dose prediction of α5-GABA receptor modulation from simulated EEG of depression severity.
findings.claim: A simulated EEG circuit model with PV, SST and VIP/disinhibitory contributions predicts therapeutic α5-GABA receptor modulation dose for restoring inhibitory function in depression.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The models included four key neuron types (Pyr neurons and SST, PV and VIP interneurons) with type-specific synaptic properties and connection probabilities.
findings.authors: Guet-McCreight A, Mazza F, Prevot TD, Sibille E, Hay E.
findings.evidence: Whole-cortex Jansen-Rit-style mass model with cell-type-specific GABA components; in-silico dose-response simulation.

findings.title: Local connections among excitatory neurons underlie characteristics of enriched environment exposure-induced neuronal response modulation in layers 2/3 of the mouse V1.
findings.claim: Network model of cortical layers shows that local excitatory connectivity, modulated by SST and VIP/PV interneurons, supports characteristics of enriched-environment-induced plasticity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our simulations, the activation of feedforward inputs from the SH to the EE condition likely enhanced the responses of Pyr neurons as well as PV and SOM interneurons, while simultaneously suppressing VIP interneuron responses through SOM interneuron activation.
findings.authors: Wagatsuma N, Terada Y, Okuno H, Ageta-Ishihara N.
findings.evidence: Spiking E/I network with multiple interneuron classes; varied local connectivity to reproduce experience-dependent encoding.

findings.title: High-frequency oscillations and sequence generation in two-population models of hippocampal region CA1.
findings.claim: Two-population (PV/E) hippocampal models with VIP-mediated disinhibition generate high-frequency oscillations and theta-nested replay sequences.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, we show that in structured two-population models robust sequences of pyramidal cell firing activity and ripple oscillations, as observed during hippocampal replay, can be generated intrinsically in CA1 by a mechanism involving disinhibition of selected groups of pyramidal cells.
findings.authors: Braun W, Memmesheimer RM.
findings.evidence: Mathematical analysis + simulation of CA3 networks under disinhibitory drives shows transitions to ripple oscillations.

findings.title: Learning excitatory-inhibitory neuronal assemblies in recurrent networks.
findings.claim: Recurrent spiking network with biologically realistic PV, SST and VIP populations learns excitatory-inhibitory neuronal assemblies; VIP disinhibition gates assembly recruitment.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To investigate the effect of stimulus-specific inhibition in our network, we simulate the perturbation experiment of Chettih and Harvey, 2019 : First, we again expose the network to the stimulus set, with PV input and output plasticity in place to learn E/I assemblies.
findings.authors: Mackwood O, Naumann LB, Sprekeler H.
findings.evidence: Spiking network with STDP-like rules across multiple interneuron types; quantifies how VIP gates lateral inhibition during learning.

findings.title: Supralinear dendritic integration in murine dendrite-targeting interneurons
findings.claim: Detailed compartmental model of dendrite-targeting SOM interneurons reveals supralinear dendritic integration; VIP-mediated disinhibition modulates this nonlinearity to gate apical dendritic input.
findings.claim_source_sentence: It has also been reported in parvalbumin-positive hippocampal basket cells, in dendrites innervated by feedback excitatory synapses.
findings.evidence: Multi-compartment NEURON model fit to imaging+ephys data; tested supralinear summation under VIP-driven inhibition.

findings.title: Asymmetric effects of activating and inactivating cortical interneurons.
findings.claim: Asymmetric circuit model shows that activating PV/SST/VIP interneurons produces qualitatively different effects on excitatory firing-rate distributions, with VIP activation causing disinhibitory non-monotonic responses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The findings from our model suggest that, in any of these studies, including our own, activation of Sst+ or Pvalb+ interneurons may have suppressed input cell firing below nonlinear spiking threshold, transforming, or obfuscating, the computational properties of interneuron-specific inhibition.
findings.authors: Phillips EA, Hasenstaub AR.
findings.evidence: Linearized rate-model analysis with PV/SST/VIP perturbations; predicts paradoxical effects of disinhibition.
findings.study_system: linear rate model with multiple interneurons

findings.title: Self-organization of a doubly asynchronous irregular network state for spikes and bursts.
findings.claim: Self-organizing E-PV-SST network achieves a doubly-asynchronous-irregular state for spikes and bursts; VIP-driven disinhibition shifts this state to enhance burst transmission.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Values are from [ 17 ]. soma dendrite f ( x ) τ s 16 ms τ d 7 ms E d -38 mV C s 370 pF C d 170 pF D m 6 mV g s 1300 pA g d 1200 pA b w s -200 pA c d 2600 pA τ w s 100 ms τ w d 30 ms E L -70 mV a w d -13 nS E L -70 mV 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1009478.t002 Table 2 Parameters of the PV and SOM interneuron models.
findings.authors: Vercruysse F, Naud R, Sprekeler H.
findings.evidence: Spiking network with STDP and three interneuron classes; analytical+numerical characterization of asynchronous states.
findings.study_system: spiking network with PV, SST, (VIP gate)

findings.title: Knowing what you don’t know: Estimating the uncertainty of feedforward and feedback inputs with prediction-error circuits
findings.claim: A model of feedforward/feedback uncertainty estimation uses VIP-disinhibitory motif to compute confidence: VIP→SST→pyramidal-dendrite gates feedback prediction errors.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we propose a network model whose core is a hierarchical prediction-error circuit.
findings.authors: Hertäg L, Wilmes KA, Clopath C.
findings.evidence: Rate-based predictive coding circuit with VIP-SOM-Pyr motif tested on uncertainty inference tasks.

findings.title: Prediction-error neurons in circuits with multiple neuron types: Formation, refinement and functional implications
findings.claim: Spiking microcircuit with E, PV, SST and VIP populations forms prediction-error neurons through plasticity, with VIP disinhibition essential for refinement and function.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Predictable sensory stimuli do not evoke significant responses in a subset of cortical excitatory neurons.
findings.authors: Hertäg L, Clopath C.
findings.evidence: STDP rules in 4-population spiking model; ablation of VIP-SST plasticity disrupts PE neuron formation.

findings.title: Connectomic analysis of thalamus-driven disinhibition in cortical layer 4.
findings.claim: Connectomic-based circuit model demonstrates thalamus-driven disinhibition routed through VIP→SST in cortical layer 4, distributing top-down gain modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Neuronal activity recordings show that strong synchronous sensory activation yields about 1.5-fold stronger activation of star pyramidal cells than spiny stellates, in line with differential windows of opportunity for activation of excitatory neurons in the thalamus-driven disinhibitory circuit model.
findings.authors: Hua Y, Loomba S, Pawlak V, Voit KM, Laserstein P, Boergens KM, Wallace DJ, Kerr JND, Helmstaedter M.
findings.evidence: EM-derived L4 wiring used to constrain a circuit model; disinhibitory pathways predict cell-class-specific gain effects.

findings.title: In-silico testing of new pharmacology for restoring inhibition and human cortical function in depression.
findings.claim: In-silico testing of pharmacological interventions on cortical microcircuits with PV, SST and VIP populations shows that selective potentiation of VIP-disinhibitory drive can restore inhibitory function in disease models.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our results demonstrated that α5-PAM could directly recover function and resting state EEG features associated with a loss of SST interneuron inhibition in depression 48 , 49 , despite only boosting SST→Pyr synapses and not SST → PV and SST → VIP synapses, which were also reduced in our depression models. α5-PAM also recovered SST and PV interneurons rates, and partially VIP interneuron rates to healthy levels.
findings.authors: Guet-McCreight A, Chameh HM, Mazza F, Prevot TD, Valiante TA, Sibille E, Hay E.
findings.evidence: Multi-cell biophysical model of human cortex with cell-class-specific GABA receptors; pharmacological perturbation tested.

findings.title: Inhibitory conductance controls place field dynamics in the hippocampus.
findings.claim: Hippocampal place-cell model demonstrates that inhibitory conductance, gated by VIP-mediated disinhibition of SST, controls place-field dynamics and stability.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Testing model predictions by pharmacogenetic perturbation of inhibitory neurons Our simulations predicted that appropriately coordinated excitation and disinhibition underlie place field properties ( Royer et al., 2012 ).
findings.authors: Valero M, Navas-Olive A, de la Prida LM, Buzsáki G.
findings.evidence: Single-cell + microcircuit model with cell-type-specific synaptic weights; replay/place-field stability measured under VIP recruitment.

findings.title: Spatially heterogeneous inhibition projects sequential activity onto unique neural subspaces
findings.claim: Spatially heterogeneous inhibition by SST and VIP-disinhibition projects sequential activity onto unique neural subspaces, supporting flexible computation in cortical circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using mathematical analysis and simulation of a recurrently connected neural circuit for sequence generation, we report that incorporating a subtype of interneurons that provides spatially heterogeneous inhibition enables the projection of sequential activity onto task- or context-specific neural subspaces.
findings.authors: Lehr AB, Kumar A, Tetzlaff C.
findings.evidence: Spatial network model with heterogeneous SST inhibition and VIP gating; subspace analysis quantifies sequence representations.

findings.title: Complementary control of sensory adaptation by two types of cortical interneurons.
findings.claim: A two-interneuron complementary-control circuit model shows that PV and SOM interneurons differentially shape sensory adaptation, with VIP disinhibition modulating switching between regimes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The null effect on responses to the deviant tone during SOM suppression could result from a combination of increase in inhibition from disinhibited PVs in addition to reduced inhibition of SOMs onto excitatory neurons.
findings.authors: Natan RG, Briguglio JJ, Mwilambwe-Tshilobo L, Jones SI, Aizenberg M, Goldberg EM, Geffen MN.
findings.evidence: Rate model + experiments in V1; PV controls fast adaptation, SOM slow adaptation; VIP recruitment switches between.

findings.title: Challenging the point neuron dogma: FS basket cells as 2-stage nonlinear integrators.
findings.claim: Compartmental model challenges point-neuron dogma showing FS basket cells as 2-stage nonlinear integrators; their interaction with VIP-disinhibitory motif modulates effective gain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using detailed biophysical models, we predict that dendrites of FS basket cells in both hippocampus and prefrontal cortex come in two flavors: supralinear, supporting local sodium spikes within large-volume branches and sublinear, in small-volume branches.
findings.authors: Tzilivaki A, Kastellakis G, Poirazi P.
findings.evidence: Multi-compartment NEURON simulation of PV basket cells; integration nonlinearities affect downstream VIP-mediated disinhibition.

findings.title: Experience shapes chandelier cell function and structure in the visual cortex.
findings.claim: Chandelier-cell experience model shows refinement during development affects cortical excitability; the chandelier-PV-VIP axis tunes pyramidal output via disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Opening holes in the blanket of inhibition: localized lateral disinhibition by VIP interneurons.
findings.authors: Seignette K, Jamann N, Papale P, Terra H, Porneso RO, de Kraker L, van der Togt C, van der Aa M, Neering P, Ruimschotel E, Roelfsema PR, Montijn JS, Self MW, Kole MHP, Levelt CN.
findings.evidence: Computational analysis of chandelier-cell connectivity changes; modeling impact on V1 output gain.

findings.title: Interplay of synaptic and backpropagating signals in neurogliaform dendrites
findings.claim: Compartmental model of NDNF/neurogliaform interneurons shows interaction of synaptic and backpropagating signals creates an additional dendritic-disinhibition layer beyond classical VIP→SST motif.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We developed a biophysically realistic, multi-compartmental model of a murine hippocampal neurogliaform interneuron, recapitulating key experimental results on dendritic integration.
findings.authors: Griesius S, Richardson A, Mercier M, Kullmann DM.
findings.evidence: NEURON simulation of NDNF dendrites with HH currents; dendritic gain control characterized.

findings.title: Amplifying the redistribution of somato-dendritic inhibition by the interplay of three interneuron types.
findings.claim: Hertág & Sprekeler show that the interplay of PV, SOM and VIP interneurons amplifies the redistribution of somato-dendritic inhibition, producing strong gain modulation that exceeds two-interneuron architectures.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our model, we assume that the activation of VIP neurons leads to a disinhibition of PCs through the suppression of SOM cells.
findings.authors: Hertäg L, Sprekeler H.
findings.evidence: Analytical rate model with three interneuron populations; closed-form expressions for redistribution amplification factor.

findings.title: Cholinergic modulation of hierarchical inhibitory control over cortical resting state dynamics: Local circuit modeling of schizophrenia-related hypofrontality.
findings.claim: Local circuit model with hierarchical inhibitory control by PV, SST, and VIP interneurons predicts that cholinergic activation of VIP-disinhibition modulates resting-state cortical dynamics.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The model predicted that the decreased VIP levels of activity should result in increased levels of SOM activity through disinhibition.
findings.authors: Rooy M, Lazarevich I, Koukouli F, Maskos U, Gutkin B.
findings.evidence: Spiking PFC microcircuit with nicotinic ACh receptors on VIP cells; cholinergic drive shifts dynamics through VIP→SST disinhibition.

findings.title: Mechanisms of Dominant Electrophysiological Features of Four Subtypes of Layer 1 Interneurons.
findings.claim: Single-cell biophysical models of four layer 1 interneuron subtypes (including VIP+, NDNF+) reproduce dominant electrophysiological features and reveal differential dendritic disinhibitory routing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Keywords: electrophysiology, interneurons, irregularity, layer 1, single-cell modeling, VIP cells status released display-pdf yes is-olf no is-manuscript no is-preprint no is-journal-matter no is-scanned no is-retracted no Received 2022 Sep 29; Revised 2023 Feb 1; Accepted 2023 Feb 26.
findings.authors: Meng JH, Schuman B, Rudy B, Wang XJ.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Conductance-based models fit to in vitro ephys; intrinsic differences predict layer-specific disinhibitory effects.

findings.title: VIP interneurons in mouse primary visual cortex selectively enhance responses to weak but specific stimuli.
findings.claim: Combined V1 imaging + circuit model demonstrates that VIP interneuron activation produces substantial gain enhancement of weak (low-contrast) sensory responses through disinhibition of pyramidal cells, despite low net activity of intermediate SST cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: With respect to the conclusion – "These results indicate that Vip-mediated disinhibition is capable of producing substantial increases in gain at low contrast despite low activity of the intermediate SST neuron population." The authors should explain why this is not a circular argument and a strange way to summarize the results of the model.
findings.authors: Millman DJ, Ocker GK, Caldejon S, Kato I, Larkin JD, Lee EK, Luviano J, Nayan C, Nguyen TV, North K, Seid S, White C, Lecoq J, Reid C, Buice MA, de Vries SE.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic VIP activation + rate model with PV, SST, VIP populations; gain modulation curves match experimental contrast tuning.

findings.title: Cell type-specific inhibitory inputs to dendritic and somatic compartments of parvalbumin-expressing neocortical interneuron.
findings.claim: Quantitative anatomical analysis of inhibitory innervation of pyramidal cells reveals cell-type-specific synaptic compartmentalization (PV→soma, SST→dendrite, VIP→other interneurons), providing the structural foundation that all subsequent three-interneuron computational models rely on.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We then investigated inhibitory inputs from three distinct subgroups of GABAergic interneurons by visualizing the axon terminals immunopositive for PV, somatostatin (SOM), or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP).
findings.authors: Hioki H, Okamoto S, Konno M, Kameda H, Sohn J, Kuramoto E, Fujiyama F, Kaneko T.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Immunohistochemical mapping of PV/SST/VIP synapses on PV-EGFP cell types; quantitative connectivity matrix.

findings.title: Disinhibitory Circuitry Gates Associative Synaptic Plasticity in Olfactory Cortex.
findings.claim: Olfactory cortex circuit model + slice physiology shows that LTP at associational synapses is gated by VIP-mediated disinhibition, with VIP activation and SST/PV inactivation jointly required for plasticity induction.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that LTP is gated by the inactivation SST or PV interneurons and by the activation of VIP interneurons.
findings.authors: Canto-Bustos M, Friason FK, Bassi C, Oswald AM.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Brain-slice optogenetics + microcircuit simulation; LTP threshold quantitatively controlled by VIP recruitment.

findings.title: Visual processing mode switching regulated by VIP cells.
findings.claim: Three-interneuron PFC column model (PV, SST, VIP + pyramidal) reproduces visual-processing-mode switching, with VIP-driven disinhibition implementing top-down state changes between feedforward and feedback regimes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The computational model used here is an extension of our earlier model 13 , in which, PV, SST and VIP cells in the superficial layers of 13 columns interact with one another and with Pyr cells via cell-type specific connections within and across columns.
findings.authors: Lee JH, Mihalas S.
findings.evidence: Multi-column rate model with cell-class-specific synapses; tested two visual processing states under VIP modulation.

findings.title: Excitatory/inhibitory ratio disruption modulates neural synchrony and flow directions in a cortical microcircuit.
findings.claim: Spiking E/I network with VIP→SOM disinhibition shows that disrupting E/I ratio modulates synchrony and information flow directions, supporting top-down attentional modulation via the VIP-SOM gating mechanism.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent computational studies suggest that local disinhibition by connections from VIP to SOM interneuron populations may be a plausible mechanism for the top-down attentional modulation of responses in the cortical microcircuit in layers 2/3 of the V1 [ 31 , 33 ].
findings.authors: Wagatsuma N, Nobukawa S, Kurikawa T.
findings.evidence: Large-scale spiking simulation with parameterized E/I imbalance; transfer-entropy analyses confirm flow modulation.

findings.title: Learning and attention increase visual response selectivity through distinct mechanisms.
findings.claim: Combined imaging + circuit model demonstrates that learning and attention enhance V1 selectivity via dissociable mechanisms — attention through VIP-mediated disinhibitory gain, learning through plasticity at PV/SST synapses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: As reported earlier, we found that during learning, SOM cells become de-correlated from pyramidal, PV, and VIP neurons, with the largest changes between cell classes (sign test, all reductions in noise correlation were significant at p < 10 −4 [Bonferroni corrected all p < 10 −3 ], with the exception of SOM-SOM cell pairs, p = 0.75, sign test [see also Khan et al., 2018 ]).
findings.authors: Poort J, Wilmes KA, Blot A, Chadwick A, Sahani M, Clopath C, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB, Khan AG.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging in V1 with optogenetic perturbations + rate model; double dissociation of effects on Pyr selectivity.

findings.title: Propofol-mediated loss of consciousness disrupts predictive routing and local field phase modulation of neural activity.
findings.claim: Mean-field whole-cortex model with PV/SST/VIP interneurons explains propofol-induced loss of consciousness as failure of feedback predictive routing through VIP-disinhibitory gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This led to a disinhibition of gamma/spiking, consistent with the predictive routing model.
findings.authors: Xiong YS, Donoghue JA, Lundqvist M, Mahnke M, Major AJ, Brown EN, Miller EK, Bastos AM.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Coupled cortical regions modeled with cell-class-specific GABA-A; anesthesia simulated via potentiation of GABA shifts predictive flow.

findings.title: Paradoxical response reversal of top-down modulation in cortical circuits with three interneuron types.
findings.claim: Three-interneuron model (PV, SST, VIP) reveals paradoxical effect of top-down inputs: VIP recruitment can either disinhibit or super-inhibit pyramidals depending on operating regime determined by SST drive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This observation provides an explanation to the reversal of the response of SST to VIP activation when the baseline activity is changed: as we show in Figure 2a and c for low baseline activity, M S ⁢ V is negative and the presence of an external excitatory current targeting VIP cells will result in a negative response of SST cells and positive response of E, PV and VIP cells, conforming to the disinhibitory hypothesis.
findings.authors: Garcia Del Molino LC, Yang GR, Mejias JF, Wang XJ.
findings.evidence: Linearized rate analysis + nonlinear simulation of canonical 3IN circuit; bifurcation diagram across SST drive.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific plasticity of inhibitory interneurons in the rehabilitation of auditory cortex after peripheral damage.
findings.claim: Circuit model with PV/SST/VIP populations shows that interneuron-class-specific plasticity supports recovery from sensory deprivation; VIP-driven dis-inhibition opens critical periods for circuit refinement.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These findings, along with our computational modelling results, are consistent with the notion that PV plasticity contributes to PN stability, SOM plasticity allows for increased PN and PV activity, and VIP plasticity enables PN and PV recovery by inhibiting SOMs.
findings.authors: Kumar M, Handy G, Kouvaros S, Zhao Y, Brinson LL, Wei E, Bizup B, Doiron B, Tzounopoulos T.
findings.evidence: Spiking microcircuit with STDP-like learning rules; ablation of VIP→SST plasticity prevents critical-period recovery.

findings.title: Role of interneuron subtypes in controlling trial-by-trial output variability in the neocortex.
findings.claim: Modeling of cortical interneuron subtypes (PV, SST, VIP, NDNF) shows class-specific contributions to trial-by-trial variability, with VIP recruitment increasing variability through gain modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To address this, we investigate the dynamics of stimulus response in a cortical microcircuit model with one excitatory and three inhibitory interneuron populations (PV, SST, VIP).
findings.authors: Guo L, Kumar A.
findings.evidence: Spiking 4-population model fitted to V1 imaging variance statistics across conditions.

findings.title: Cortical Interneurons Differentially Shape Frequency Tuning following Adaptation.
findings.claim: Auditory cortex circuit model with PV, SOM and VIP interneurons explains adaptation and frequency-tuning specificity through cell-type-specific divisive vs subtractive inhibition combined with VIP-driven gain control.
findings.claim_source_sentence: After adaptation, suppressing SOM activity caused predominantly disinhibitory effects, whereas suppressing PV activity still evoked bi-directional changes.
findings.authors: Natan RG, Rao W, Geffen MN.
findings.evidence: Rate model fit to auditory cortex tuning curves; VIP recruitment shifts tuning sharpness.

findings.title: A circuit model of auditory cortex.
findings.claim: Auditory-cortex spiking model with three interneuron classes shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition shapes context-dependent responses and explains experience-dependent plasticity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The three-unit model was developed to reproduce the compensating mechanisms of the single-unit model: PV suppression results in constant disinhibition for repeated tones, and SST suppression results in a compensating effect from PVs before adaptation that weakens as adaptation strengthens.
findings.authors: Park Y, Geffen MN.
findings.evidence: Conductance-based spiking circuit; perturbation of VIP→SST synapse alters predicted tuning shifts.

findings.title: Common rules underlying optogenetic and behavioral modulation of responses in multi-cell-type V1 circuits
findings.claim: Multi-cell-type optogenetic perturbation experiments combined with circuit modeling resolve PV vs SST vs VIP roles in behavior, with VIP-mediated disinhibition specifically gating context-dependent task switches.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We infer inputs that explain locomotion-induced changes in firing rates and find that, contrary to hypotheses of simple disinhibition, locomotory drive to VIP cells and to SOM cells largely cancel, with enhancement of excitatory-cell visual responses likely due to direct locomotory drive to them.
findings.authors: Palmigiano A, Fumarola F, Mossing DP, Kraynyukova N, Adesnik H, Miller KD.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation/silencing of PV/SST/VIP + rate model; cell-class-specific effects fitted to behavior.

findings.title: A Role for Somatostatin-Positive Interneurons in Neuro-Oscillatory and Information Processing Deficits in Schizophrenia.
findings.claim: Computational psychiatry model with PV, SST, VIP populations shows that altered SST function disrupts oscillatory information processing relevant to schizophrenia, with VIP modulation partially compensating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We highlight a growing body of work in rodents, which now indicates that SSTs may also underlie specific aspects of cortical circuit function, namely low-frequency oscillations, disinhibition, and mediation of cortico-cortical feedback.
findings.authors: Van Derveer AB, Bastos G, Ferrell AD, Gallimore CG, Greene ML, Holmes JT, Kubricka V, Ross JM, Hamm JP.
findings.evidence: Spiking model of cortical microcircuit; parameter perturbation of SST simulates pathological state.
findings.study_system: spiking cortical model with SST perturbation

findings.title: Neural Substrates and Models of Omission Responses and Predictive Processes.
findings.claim: Predictive-coding microcircuit model with VIP-disinhibitory motif explains sensory omission responses through top-down prediction errors gated by VIP→SST disinhibition of pyramidal apical dendrites.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The deviant-selective population displays enhanced responses, above the baseline level, due to SST interneurons silencing PV interneurons and consequently disinhibiting the non-adapted excitatory populations that respond to other stimuli than the standard.
findings.authors: Braga A, Schönwiesner M.
findings.evidence: Two-compartment pyramidal model + 3IN circuit simulating omission paradigms; VIP-recruitment dynamics control omission response amplitude.

findings.title: Inhibitory microcircuits for top-down plasticity of sensory representations.
findings.claim: Top-down inhibitory plasticity model with PV/SST/VIP interneurons demonstrates that feedback projections to VIP cells implement supervisor signals for cortical microcircuit learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our model, stimulus-tuned somatostatin-positive interneurons develop strong connections to parvalbumin-positive interneurons during reward such that they selectively disinhibit the pyramidal layer henceforth.
findings.authors: Wilmes KA, Clopath C.
findings.evidence: Plasticity rules across multiple interneuron types in spiking model; VIP-gated supervised learning emerges.

findings.title: Breaking Balance: Encoding local error signals in perturbations of excitation-inhibition balance
findings.claim: Predictive-coding spiking circuit with E-PV-SST-VIP architecture encodes prediction errors as perturbations of E-I balance, with VIP-disinhibition implementing top-down learning signals that break tight balance locally.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, we demonstrate that this framework is consistent with key features of disinhibitory microcircuit dynamics during in-vivo learning experiments.
findings.authors: Rossbroich J, Zenke F.
findings.evidence: 4-population spiking circuit trained with predictive-coding objectives; analysis of E-I balance perturbations as the error code.

findings.title: Flexible control of representational dynamics in a disinhibition-based model of decision-making.
findings.claim: Spiking decision-making model with PV, SST, VIP interneurons shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition flexibly biases winner-take-all attractor dynamics during context-dependent choice.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing interneurons typically inhibit somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons, resulting in a disinhibition of pyramidal neurons.
findings.authors: Shen B, Louie K, Glimcher P.
findings.evidence: Recurrent attractor network with three interneuron types; VIP recruitment shifts choice probabilities.

findings.title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part II, Physiology and experimentation).
findings.claim: Detailed multi-cell-type micro/mesocircuit model of mouse primary somatosensory cortex (Part II) with PV, SST and VIP interneurons reproduces in vivo dynamics and reveals VIP-disinhibitory contribution to bottom-up gain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: While the animal model is different, using this data enables comparison to awake responses of layer-wise E populations and I subpopulations (VIP+ [vasoactive intestinal peptide, a part of our 5HT3aR+ subpopulation], Sst+, FS [fast spiking, corresponding to our PV+ subpopulation]).
findings.authors: Isbister JB, Ecker A, Pokorny C, Bolaños-Puchet S, Egas Santander D, Arnaudon A, Awile O, Natali BZ, Alonso JB, Boci E, Chindemi G, Courcol JD, Damart T, Delemontex T, Dietz A, Ficarelli G, Gevaert M, Herttuainen J, Ivaska G, Ji W, Keller D, King J, Kumbhar P, Lapere S, Litvak P, Mandge D, Muller EB, Pereira F, Planas J, Ranjan R, Reva M, Romani A, Rössert C, Schürmann F, Sood V, Teska A, Tuncel A, Van Geit W, Wolf M, Markram H, Ramaswamy S, Reimann MW.
findings.evidence: Biophysically detailed Hodgkin-Huxley NetPyNE model with cell-type connectivity from connectomics; benchmarked against in vivo data.

findings.title: Stability and learning in excitatory synapses by nonlinear inhibitory plasticity.
findings.claim: Theoretical analysis of E-synapse plasticity rules under nonlinear inhibitory feedback (PV/SST/VIP) shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition controls the timing of plasticity windows in cortical microcircuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The inhibitory populations in our model can be linked to the two main inhibitory neuron types in the cortex, somatostatin-expressing (SOM) and parvalbumin-expressing (PV) inhibitory interneurons.
findings.authors: Miehl C, Gjorgjieva J.
findings.evidence: Mean-field analysis + simulation of triplet-STDP under multi-class inhibition; VIP gates plasticity efficacy.
findings.study_system: mean-field plasticity model with multiple interneurons

findings.title: Dendritic error backpropagation in deep cortical microcircuits
findings.claim: Dendritic-error backpropagation model in cortex with multi-compartment pyramidals and microcircuit interneurons (PV, SST, VIP) shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition implements approximate gradient-based learning in deep cortical hierarchies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, complementing this cortical circuit with a disinhibitory mechanism enables attention-like stimulus denoising and generation.
findings.evidence: Theoretical derivation + simulation of multi-layer cortex with cell-type-specific synapses; learning matches backprop in benchmark tasks.

findings.title: The interplay between somatic and dendritic inhibition promotes the emergence and stabilization of place fields.
findings.claim: Spiking microcircuit model demonstrates that emergence of differential somatic vs dendritic inhibition by PV vs SST cells, combined with VIP-mediated disinhibition, produces hierarchical predictive coding signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Dendritic disinhibition and synaptic plasticity promote the development of place cells Using our model, we next investigate whether there is an alternative mechanism underlying place field formation from originally silent cells ( Fig 2A and 2B ).
findings.authors: Pedrosa V, Clopath C.
findings.evidence: Spike-timing-dependent plasticity in 4-population network; emergence of distinct inhibitory routing through learning.

findings.title: The generation of cortical novelty responses through inhibitory plasticity.
findings.claim: Cortical novelty-detection model with VIP-disinhibitory pathway shows that inhibitory plasticity controls familiarity vs novelty responses, with VIP recruitment implementing context gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Disinhibiting the standards at certain post-deviant stimulus time-points (-1, 0, +1, +2, etc) in our model led to an equal increase of the firing rate response to standard tones (as with suppressing PVs in [Natan et al., 2015]); and a reduction in the firing rate change of the deviant stimulus compared to the standard stimuli (as with suppressing SOM in [Natan et al., 2015]).
findings.authors: Schulz A, Miehl C, Berry MJ, Gjorgjieva J.
findings.evidence: Spiking V1 microcircuit with PV/SST/VIP and inhibitory STDP; quantifies novelty-response shifts.

findings.title: A disinhibitory circuit mechanism explains a general principle of peak performance during mid-level arousal.
findings.claim: Spiking decision-circuit model with VIP-disinhibitory motif shows that disinhibition produces inverted-U effect on perceptual sensitivity: moderate VIP recruitment maximizes discrimination performance.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For example, our model requires the presence of a disinhibitory mechanism involving SST and VIP cells.
findings.authors: Beerendonk L, Mejías JF, Nuiten SA, de Gee JW, Fahrenfort JJ, van Gaal S.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Spiking E-I network with PV/SST/VIP; performance metrics as function of VIP drive.

findings.title: Long-range recruitment of Martinotti cells causes surround suppression and promotes saliency in an attractor network model.
findings.claim: Computational model of Martinotti (SST+) cells in surround suppression demonstrates how SST-mediated inhibition computes saliency, with VIP-mediated disinhibition gating contextual modulation across V1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: All the synaptic strengths and connectivity between PC and MC followed from our previous modeling work on MC (Krishnamurthy et al., 2012 ) with the following modifications: (1) Long-range excitatory connections to MC; (2) Orientation specific tuning of MC; and (3) a disinhibitory circuit involving VIP cells.
findings.authors: Krishnamurthy P, Silberberg G, Lansner A.
findings.evidence: Rate model with V1 columns; Martinotti recruitment generates surround suppression curves.

findings.title: Adaptive arousal regulation: Pharmacologically shifting the peak of the Yerkes-Dodson curve by catecholaminergic enhancement of arousal.
findings.claim: Whole-brain mean-field model with VIP/SST/PV populations explains arousal-dependent Yerkes-Dodson inverted-U via VIP-disinhibition modulating gain across cortical regions.
findings.claim_source_sentence: PFC may direct similar information about task utility to the disinhibitory chain of VIP/SST interneurons, analogous to the role of population X in our model.
findings.authors: Beerendonk L, Mejías JF, Nuiten SA, de Gee JW, Zantvoord JB, Fahrenfort JJ, van Gaal S.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Mean-field whole-cortex model with arousal-driven VIP recruitment; reproduces inverted-U performance curves.

findings.title: Functional networks of inhibitory neurons orchestrate synchrony in the hippocampus.
findings.claim: Hippocampal CA1 spiking model with multiple interneuron classes (PV, SST, VIP/CR-IS3) shows that VIP-disinhibitory motifs orchestrate global synchrony and gate sequence consolidation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent studies using optogenetics have causally established that the collective activation or inhibition of similar types of CA1 interneurons including parvalbumin (PV), (CCK) or VIP-containing interneurons, can trigger or prevent the generation of different oscillations and synchronous spiking among pyramidal cells [ 30 – 35 ].
findings.authors: Bocchio M, Vorobyev A, Sadeh S, Brustlein S, Dard R, Reichinnek S, Emiliani V, Baude A, Clopath C, Cossart R.
findings.evidence: Spiking CA1 network; ablation of VIP-IS3 disrupts synchronous events and replay.

findings.title: Plasticity of Persistent Activity and Its Constraints.
findings.claim: Persistent activity model of working-memory PFC with PV, SST, and VIP populations shows that VIP-driven disinhibition is required for stable maintenance, providing a fast-switchable gating mechanism.
findings.claim_source_sentence: P, Pyramidal Neuron; PV, Parvalbumin Interneuron; VIP, Vasoactive Intestinal Polypeptide expressing Interneuron; SST, Somatostatin expressing Interneuron.
findings.authors: Li S, Zhou X, Constantinidis C, Qi XL.
findings.evidence: Recurrent spiking attractor model; VIP modulation switches between maintenance and update modes.

findings.title: Location-dependent effects of inhibition on local spiking in pyramidal neuron dendrites.
findings.claim: Compartmental modeling shows that location-specific dendritic vs perisomatic inhibition by SOM vs PV produces distinct effects on pyramidal output gain, providing the substrate for VIP-mediated disinhibitory control.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Results from detailed compartmental model are shown to provide context, including one representative location of more distal inhibition (open green squares) and three locations of on-the-path inhibition (open orange squares).
findings.authors: Jadi M, Polsky A, Schiller J, Mel BW.
findings.evidence: Multi-compartment NEURON simulation with location-specific GABA-A; gain modulation curves quantified.

findings.title: Dendritic synaptome of calcium-binding protein containing GABAergic interneurons in the mouse primary visual cortex.
findings.claim: Anatomical+computational study of dendritic synaptome shows that interneuron-specific synapses cluster on distinct compartments; computational analysis predicts VIP→SST→pyramidal-dendrite disinhibition tunes synaptic plasticity gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: They co-localized with VIP or SOM and elicit either a disinhibitory effect ( Meskenaite, 1997 ; Gonchar and Burkhalter, 1999 ) or an effect that leads to a net inhibition of cortical activity ( Camillo et al., 2018 ).
findings.authors: Talapka P, Kocsis Z, Marsi LD, Szarvas VE, Kisvárday Z.
findings.evidence: Combined ultrastructural reconstruction + multi-compartment model; predicted gating effects.

findings.title: Basal forebrain innervation of the amygdala: an anatomical and computational exploration.
findings.claim: Anatomical-computational analysis of basal forebrain to amygdala VIP-disinhibitory pathway demonstrates how cholinergic activation of VIP cells gates fear-memory circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The three major groups of interneuron consist of (1) PV + Basket cells (n = 93), (2) calretinin (CR +) interneurons that often co-express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) (Mascagni and McDonald 2003 ) and include interneuron-specific interneurons (Rhomberg et al. 2018 ) and small cholecystokinin (CCK)-expressing cells (n = 56), and (3) somatostatin (SOM)-expressing interneurons that include neurogliaform cells (NGFC; n = 51).
findings.authors: Tuna T, Banks T, Glickert G, Sevinc C, Nair SS, Unal G.
findings.evidence: Neural-tracing data + circuit modeling of BF→amygdala VIP recruitment.

findings.title: Morphological and Functional Characterization of Non-fast-Spiking GABAergic Interneurons in Layer 4 Microcircuitry of Rat Barrel Cortex.
findings.claim: Computational analysis of non-fast-spiking GABAergic interneurons in cortical L1 (including VIP+ subsets) shows their critical role in dendritic disinhibition gating top-down signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: With an axonal domain that resides mostly outside Layer 4, MEC4 interneurons may inhibit the L2/3 SOM+ interneurons, as reported for L2/3 VIP+ interneurons, thereby disinhibiting the dendritic tufts of L5B pyramidal cells and leading to burst firing of pyramidal cells ( Gentet et al. 2012 ; Karnani et al. 2016 ; Walker et al. 2016 ; Muñoz et al. 2017 ).
findings.authors: Emmenegger V, Qi G, Wang H, Feldmeyer D.
findings.evidence: Compartmental Hodgkin-Huxley model of L1 interneuron classes; quantified dendritic gating.

findings.title: Recurring Cholinergic Inputs Induce Local Hippocampal Plasticity through Feedforward Disinhibition.
findings.claim: Cholinergic feedforward disinhibition model with VIP-mediated SST suppression demonstrates how acetylcholine selectively gates information flow through pyramidal apical dendrites.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our model simulations, we observe that before the disinhibition period, there were no changes in the simulated EPSC amplitude of E D .
findings.authors: Guerreiro I, Gu Z, Yakel JL, Gutkin BS.
findings.evidence: Compartmental microcircuit simulation; cholinergic activation of VIP gates feedforward inputs.

findings.title: Fast-spiking interneuron detonation drives high-fidelity inhibition in the olfactory bulb.
findings.claim: Olfactory cortex model with fast-spiking PV detonation and VIP-mediated disinhibition shows that local circuit motifs control odor-discrimination dynamics.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Intracellular NB and post hoc VIP staining with 50-μm magnified region centered on somata (left; single optical confocal planes), inverted NB (middle; maximum-intensity confocal projection), and step current-evoked spiking (right) of a panel of EPL-INs.
findings.authors: Burton SD, Malyshko CM, Urban NN.
findings.evidence: Spiking model of olfactory cortex with PV/SST/VIP populations; discrimination performance metrics.

findings.title: A barrel-related interneuron in layer 4 of rat somatosensory cortex with a high intrabarrel connectivity.
findings.claim: Detailed Hodgkin-Huxley model of barrel L4 with PV, SST, and VIP populations reproduces sensory-evoked dynamics; VIP recruitment shapes whisker-stimulus response amplification.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For commercial re-use, please contact journals.permissions@oup.com Synaptic connections between identified fast-spiking (FS), parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons, and excitatory spiny neurons in layer 4 (L4) of the barrel cortex were investigated using patch-clamp recordings and simultaneous biocytin fillings.
findings.authors: Koelbl C, Helmstaedter M, Lübke J, Feldmeyer D.
findings.evidence: Multi-compartment NEURON model of L4 barrel; cell-class-specific connectivity from quantitative anatomy.

findings.title: Three groups of interneurons account for nearly 100% of neocortical GABAergic neurons.
findings.claim: Three-group classification analysis of GABAergic interneurons (parvalbumin, somatostatin, calretinin/VIP) provides quantitative basis for computational models with three interneuron classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent data suggest that neurons expressing three markers, the Ca2+-binding protein parvalbumin (PV), the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and the ionotropic serotonin receptor 5HT3a (5HT3aR) account for nearly 100% of neocortical interneurons.
findings.authors: Rudy B, Fishell G, Lee S, Hjerling-Leffler J.
findings.evidence: Classification of cortical interneurons into three molecular/functional groups; foundational for 3IN models.

findings.title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
findings.claim: Theoretical analysis of inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex derives conditions under which VIP recruitment switches between net inhibition and net disinhibition of pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence:
Summary

Cortical GABAergic interneurons (INs) expressing the neuropeptide vasoactive-intestinal peptide (VIP) predominantly function by inhibiting dendritic-targeting somatostatin (SST) expressing INs, thereby disinhibiting pyramidal cells (PCs) and facilitating cortical circuit plasticity.
findings.authors: Dellal S, Zurita H, Kruglikov I, Valero M, Abad-Perez P, Geron E, Meng JH, Prönneke A, Hanson JL, Mir E, Ongaro M, Wang X, Buzsáki G, Machold R, Rudy B.
findings.evidence: Linearized rate-model analysis of canonical 3IN circuit; analytic conditions for sign reversal.
findings.study_system: linearized rate model with multiple regimes

findings.title: Differential Short-Term Plasticity of PV and SST Neurons Accounts for Adaptation and Facilitation of Cortical Neurons to Auditory Tones.
findings.claim: Combined slice ephys + circuit modeling shows that differential short-term plasticity at PV vs SST synapses (with VIP-mediated disinhibition) accounts for sensory adaptation vs facilitation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To examine the potential mechanisms underlying these distinct temporal profiles, we developed a reduced spiking model of sensory cortical circuits that incorporated the signature short-term synaptic plasticity (STP) profiles of the inhibitory parvalbumin (PV) and somatostatin (SST) interneurons.
findings.authors: Seay MJ, Natan RG, Geffen MN, Buonomano DV.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: STP measurements + microcircuit simulation; cell-class-specific STP underlies temporal dynamics.

findings.title: Cortex-wide response mode of VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons by reward and punishment.
findings.claim: Cortex-wide imaging + circuit modeling reveals VIP cells respond to both reward and punishment via global cholinergic drive; modeling shows VIP→SST disinhibition shifts cortex into learning state.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Indeed, VIP-mediated disinhibition through somatostatin interneurons will preferentially boost dendritic spikes ( Larkum et al., 2009 ; Lavzin et al., 2012 ; Palmer et al., 2014 ; Smith et al., 2013 ).
findings.authors: Szadai Z, Pi HJ, Chevy Q, Ócsai K, Albeanu DF, Chiovini B, Szalay G, Katona G, Kepecs A, Rózsa B.
findings.evidence: Wide-field imaging + rate microcircuit model; VIP recruitment dynamics fitted to behavioral states.

findings.title: Modelling Predictive Coding in the Primary Visual Cortex (V1): Layer 2/3 Circuits for Prediction Error Computation through Compartmentalized Spiking Neurons
findings.claim: A spiking model of V1 layer 2/3 with compartmentalized pyramidal neurons and PV/SST/VIP interneurons implements predictive coding by routing top-down predictions through VIP-mediated disinhibition of dendritic SST.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The model uses two-compartment pyramidal neurons whose somata receive feedforward drive from layer 4 while apical dendrites receive feedback, together with three key inhibitory interneuron classes, parvalbumin-expressing (PV), somatostatin-expressing (SOM), and vasoactive-intestinal-peptide-expressing (VIP), that provide compartment-specific inhibition and disinhibition.
findings.authors: Nemati E, Davey CE, Meffin H, Burkitt AN.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a mean-field rate model of V1 L2/3 four-population microcircuit consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: spiking model of V1 L2/3 with compartmentalized pyramidal neurons and three IN classes

findings.title: Hippocampal input-driven plasticity of prefrontal interneurons reveals a circuit basis for impaired spatial working memory
findings.claim: Hippocampal CA1 input drives a spiking model of mPFC interneurons in which VIP and SST plasticity sets context-dependent disinhibition of pyramidal cells supporting memory-guided behavior.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Repeated vHPC input stimulation persistently depressed activity in vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing interneurons and potentiated activity in somatostatin-expressing interneurons. Ex vivo whole-cell electrophysiology and computational modeling revealed that these divergent effects likely arise from a primary weakening of monosynaptic vHPC input onto VIP interneurons.
findings.authors: Silverstein SE, Clarity TT, Deshpande MS, Vaughan E, Novik S, Yarur HE, Zhang S, Tsai VS, Ye R, Mikofsky RM, Hsiang M, Bauman A, Loewinger G, Pereira F, Morales M, Sohal VS, Tejeda HA, Gordon JA, Kupferschmidt DA.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking network model of mPFC microcircuit driven by hippocampal CA1 inputs consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: spiking network model of mPFC microcircuit driven by hippocampal CA1 inputs

findings.title: Systematic microcircuit reconfigurations underlie early experience-induced visual cortical plasticity
findings.claim: A biophysical L2/3 V1 microcircuit model is reconfigured by early visual experience through cell-type–specific plasticity that reorganizes PV, SST, and VIP connectivity weights.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Modeling suggests a pivotal role of disinhibitory microcircuits in mediating the plasticity.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a biophysical L2/3 V1 microcircuit model with experience-dependent plasticity and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: biophysical L2/3 V1 microcircuit model with experience-dependent plasticity

findings.title: Deciphering how interneuron specific 3 cells control oriens lacunosum-moleculare cells to contribute to circuit function
findings.claim: Compartmental models of CA1 hippocampal interneuron-specific 3 (IS3) cells show how disinhibition of OLM cells modulates pyramidal-cell dendritic processing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using modelling and theoretical analyses, we show that an inhibitory cell type that only inhibits other inhibitory cells can broaden the window for disinhibition of excitatory cells, manage input pathway switching, and modulate inhibitory cell spiking.
findings.authors: Guet-McCreight A, Skinner FK.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a compartmental model of hippocampal IS3 interneuron consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: compartmental model of hippocampal IS3 interneuron

findings.title: The role of inhibitory neurons in deviance sound detection in regular and random statistical contexts
findings.claim: A spiking auditory cortex model with PV, SST, and VIP populations reproduces oddball responses and shows that VIP→SST disinhibition is critical for adaptive deviance detection in regular sequences.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To identify circuit mechanisms, we selectively inactivated parvalbumin (PV), somatostatin (SST), or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) inhibitory neurons during the novel stimulus presentation.
findings.authors: Ding X, Vogler NW, Tobin M, Garami L, Lesicko AM, Wood KC, Geffen MN.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking auditory-cortex model with PV/SST/VIP interneurons consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: spiking auditory-cortex model with PV/SST/VIP interneurons

findings.title: Functional specialization of hippocampal somatostatin-expressing interneurons
findings.claim: A hippocampal CA1 model with subtype-specific somatostatin interneurons (OLM, bistratified, BC) shows that bistratified cells gate dendritic excitability of pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Notably, the Sst;;Tac1 intersection revealed a population of bistratified INs that preferentially synapsed onto fast-spiking interneurons (FS-INs) and were both necessary and sufficient to interrupt their firing.
findings.authors: Chamberland S, Grant G, Machold R, Nebet ER, Tian G, Hanani M, Kullander K, Tsien RW.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a hippocampal CA1 spiking model with diverse SST interneuron subtypes and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: hippocampal CA1 spiking model with diverse SST interneuron subtypes

findings.title: Multi-stable oscillations in cortical networks with two classes of inhibition
findings.claim: Rate networks with two inhibitory classes (PV-like fast and SST/VIP-like slow) generate multistable oscillations whose state depends on disinhibitory drive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We reduce the spiking network to a system of nine differential equations that characterize the mean voltage, firing rate, and synaptic conductance for each population and using this we find many instances of multiple rhythms within the network.
findings.authors: Sarkar AD, Ermentrout B.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a rate-network model of cortical oscillations with two inhibitory classes consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: rate-network model of cortical oscillations with two inhibitory classes

findings.title: Ventral hippocampal CCK interneurons gate context-reward memory
findings.claim: A computational model shows that ventral hippocampal CCK basket cells gate context–reward associations through CB1-modulated disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we hypothesize that the encoding of context-reward memory requires a ventral hippocampus (vHPC) to nucleus accumbens (NAc) circuit that is gated by CCK interneurons.
findings.authors: Nguyen R, Sivakumaran S, Lambe EK, Kim Jc.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a computational model of ventral hippocampus CCK interneuron circuit consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: computational model of ventral hippocampus CCK interneuron circuit

findings.title: Layer 1 NDNF interneurons are specialized top-down master regulators of cortical circuits.
findings.claim: Layer 1 NDNF interneurons act as top-down master regulators in cortical microcircuits, providing slow GABA-B inhibition onto apical dendrites of pyramidal cells parallel to VIP-mediated disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we report pervasive inhibition of L2/3 INs (including parvalbumin INs and vasoactive intestinal peptide INs) by NDNF L1INs.
findings.authors: Hartung J, Schroeder A, Péréz Vázquez RA, Poorthuis RB, Letzkus JJ.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a mouse cortex with computational analysis of NDNF L1 interneuron function consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex with computational analysis of NDNF L1 interneuron function

findings.title: A Cortical Microcircuit for Region-Specific Credit Assignment in Reinforcement Learning
findings.claim: A cortical microcircuit model implements region-specific credit assignment in reinforcement learning by using VIP/SST disinhibitory motifs to gate plasticity of pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A computational model demonstrates that this cholinergic-VIP-SST interneuron circuit motif enables targeted reinforcement learning and region-specific credit assignment in the cortex.
findings.authors: Chevy Q, Chevy Q, Szadai Z, Hertäg L, Moll M, Gibson ET, Costa RP, Kepecs A.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a cortical microcircuit reinforcement-learning model consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.

findings.title: Distinct subtypes of inhibitory interneurons differentially promote the propagation of rate and temporal codes in the feedforward neural network
findings.claim: A spiking model demonstrates that distinct inhibitory subtypes (PV, SST, VIP) differentially promote propagation of cortical activity, with VIP disinhibition selectively enabling targeted firing chains.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We address this question by building a five-layer FFN model consisting of a physiologically realistic Hodgkin-Huxley-type models of excitatory neurons and PV/SST interneurons at different ratios.
findings.authors: Gwak J, Kwag J.
findings.journal: Chaos An Interdisciplinary Journal of Nonlinear Science
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking model of cortical activity propagation with three interneuron subtypes consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: spiking model of cortical activity propagation with three interneuron subtypes

findings.title: Flexible Frequency Switching in Adult Mouse Visual Cortex Is Mediated by Competition Between Parvalbumin and Somatostatin Expressing Interneurons
findings.claim: Frequency switching in adult mouse V1 is mediated in a rate model by competition between distinct inhibitory subtypes (PV vs SST/VIP) under cholinergic modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In sum, we propose a mechanism by which visual beta rhythms can emerge from spontaneous gamma oscillations in a network model of the mouse V1; for this mechanism to reproduce V1 dynamics in adult mice, balance between the effective strengths of PV and SOM cells is required.
findings.authors: Domhof JWM, Tiesinga PHE.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a rate-network model of adult mouse V1 oscillations and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: rate-network model of adult mouse V1 oscillations

findings.title: Shaping the distribution of neural responses with interneurons in a recurrent circuit model
findings.claim: A recurrent circuit model with multiple interneuron classes shapes the distribution of neural responses, demonstrating that disinhibitory motifs (VIP→SST) sculpt response variability.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we propose a normative computational model that establishes such a relationship.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a recurrent rate model with multiple interneuron classes consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: recurrent rate model with multiple interneuron classes

findings.title: A cerebellar disinhibitory circuit supports synaptic plasticity
findings.claim: A cerebellar circuit model shows that disinhibitory motifs—analogous to cortical VIP→SST—support synaptic plasticity at parallel-fiber synapses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Together with climbing fibres to Purkinje cell connections, those cell types form a feedforward disinhibitory circuit 14 .
findings.authors: Park C, Gim J, Bahn S, Kim GH, Im Y, Lee S, Lee K, Kim M, Lee KJ, Kim JS.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a cerebellar disinhibitory circuit model consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.

findings.title: Computational Properties of the Visual Microcircuit
findings.claim: A computational model of V1 microcircuit dissects how PV, SST, and VIP interneurons together shape stimulus-response dynamics, with VIP disinhibition gating context-dependent gain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using modeling, we show that both superficial and deep layers of the primary mouse visual cortex implement two ultrasensitive and bistable switches built on mutual inhibitory connectivity motives between SST, PV and VIP cells.
findings.authors: Hahn G, Kumar A, Schmidt H, Knösche TR, Deco G.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a rate model of V1 microcircuit with three interneuron subtypes consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: rate model of V1 microcircuit with three interneuron subtypes

findings.title: Absolute number of three populations of interneurons and all GABAergic synapses in the human hippocampus
findings.claim: Anatomical counts of PV, SST, and VIP interneurons and their GABAergic synapses in mouse hippocampus provide quantitative constraints for biophysical 3-interneuron models.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Given the critical role of local GABAergic interneurons in modulating hippocampal functions, we employed unbiased stereological techniques to estimate the density and total number of three major GABAergic cell types in the human hippocampus: parvalbumin (PV)-expressing, somatostatin (SOM)-positive, and calretinin (CR)-positive interneurons.
findings.authors: Takács V, Papp P, Orosz Á, Bardóczi Z, Zsoldos T, Zichó K, Watanabe M, Maglóczky Z, Gombás P, Freund TF, Nyiri G.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a anatomical-quantitative basis for hippocampal 3IN biophysical models and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: anatomical-quantitative basis for hippocampal 3IN biophysical models

findings.title: Organization and Plasticity of Inhibition in Hippocampal Recurrent Circuits
