findings.claim: A hippocampal recurrent circuit model with PV, SST, VIP, and CCK interneurons shows that disinhibitory plasticity organizes the assignment of inhibition during memory encoding.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the CA3 area of the hippocampus, recurrent circuit dynamics, including experience-induced plasticity at excitatory synapses, are thought to play a key role in episodic memory encoding and consolidation via rapid generation and flexible selection of neural ensembles.
findings.authors: Vancura B, Geiller T, Losonczy A.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a hippocampal recurrent circuit model with multiple interneuron subtypes consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: hippocampal recurrent circuit model with multiple interneuron subtypes

findings.title: A circuit mechanism for independent modulation of excitatory and inhibitory firing rates after sensory deprivation
findings.claim: A circuit mechanism modeled with PV, SST, and VIP interneurons enables independent modulation of excitatory and inhibitory firing rates via VIP-mediated disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that with a single interneuron subtype (parvalbumin-expressing [PV]), excitatory and inhibitory firing rates can only be comodulated-increased or decreased together.
findings.authors: Richter LMA, Gjorgjieva J.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
findings.evidence: Authors developed a rate model of cortical microcircuit with three interneuron subtypes consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: rate model of cortical microcircuit with three interneuron subtypes

findings.title: NeoCOMM: A Neocortical Neuroinspired Computational Model for the Reconstruction and Simulation of Epileptiform Events
findings.claim: NeoCOMM is a neuroinspired computational neocortical model that incorporates PV, SST, and VIP interneurons to reconstruct multi-scale cortical activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our model allowed us to unravel the relationship between glutamatergic and GABAergic synaptic transmission of the epileptic neural network and the type of generated IEE.
findings.authors: Al Harrach M, Yochum M, Ruffini G, Bartolomei F, Benquet P, Wendling F.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a large-scale neocortical computational model and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.

findings.title: Oscillation-Driven Memory Encoding, Maintenance, and Recall in an Entorhinal–Hippocampal Circuit Model
findings.claim: A spiking model of the entorhinal-hippocampal circuit reproduces oscillation-driven memory encoding and recall via interneuron-mediated disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To explore such mechanisms in spatial working memory, we constructed a neural network model involving parvalbumin-positive, somatostatin-positive, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-positive interneurons in the hippocampal CA1 and the superficial and deep layers of medial entorhinal cortex (MEC).
findings.authors: Kurikawa T, Mizuseki K, Fukai T.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking entorhinal-hippocampal memory circuit model consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.

findings.title: Layer 1 NDNF+ Interneurons Control Bilateral Sensory Processing in a Layer-dependent Manner
findings.claim: A computational model of layer 1 NDNF+ interneurons shows that they control bilateral sensory processing by providing layer-dependent dendritic inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Direct connections from the contralateral cortex and the ipsilateral side activate NDNF+ neurons, which subsequently inhibit the late spiking activity of underlying layer 2/3 neurons, but not layer 5.
findings.authors: Vighagen R, Gesuita L, Damilou A, Cavaccini A, Banterle L, Steenhuis V, Karayannis T.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a cortical model of L1 NDNF+ interneuron control of sensory processing and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: cortical model of L1 NDNF+ interneuron control of sensory processing

findings.title: Model of the HVC neural network as a song motor in zebra finch
findings.claim: A spiking neural network model of the zebra finch HVC—including inhibitory interneurons—reproduces song motor sequencing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that this network model qualitatively exhibits observed electrophysiological behaviors of neurons independent or in the network, as well as the importance of bidirectional interactions between the HVCRA neuron and the HVCI neuron.
findings.authors: Xia P, Abarbanel HDI.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking model of zebra finch HVC song motor circuit and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: spiking model of zebra finch HVC song motor circuit

findings.title: Entorhinal cortex glutamatergic and GABAergic projections bidirectionally control discrimination and generalization of hippocampal representations
findings.claim: Entorhinal cortex glutamatergic and GABAergic projections bidirectionally control hippocampal computations through models that include VIP/SST disinhibitory motifs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, conjunctively active LEC <sub>GABA</sub> suppresses this local inhibition to disinhibit CA3 pyramidal neuron soma and selectively boost integrative output of LEC and CA3 recurrent network.
findings.authors: Robert V, O’Neil K, Rashid SK, Johnson CD, De La Torre RG, Zemelman BV, Clopath C, Basu J.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a entorhinal-hippocampal circuit model consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific synaptic scaling mechanisms differentially contribute to associative learning
findings.claim: Cell-type–specific synaptic scaling on PV, SST, and VIP interneurons differentially contributes to associative memory in a spiking microcircuit model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Specifically, parvalbumin (PV)-expressing inhibitory neurons, targeting perisomatic regions of excitatory (E) pyramidal neurons, and somatostatin (SST)-expressing neurons, targeting distal dendrites, exhibit distinct scaling responses.
findings.authors: Veneto F, Kepçe A, Wu YK, Gjorgjieva J.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking memory model with cell-type-specific synaptic scaling and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: spiking memory model with cell-type-specific synaptic scaling

findings.title: Cell-type specific contributions to theta-gamma coupled rhythms in the hippocampus
findings.claim: A hippocampal CA1 spiking model demonstrates that PV-, SST-, and CCK-expressing interneurons make distinct cell-type-specific contributions to theta-gamma coupled rhythms.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that CCK-expressing basket cells initiate the coupled rhythms and regularize theta, and PV-expressing basket cells enhance both theta and gamma rhythms.
findings.authors: Sengupta S, Talidou A, Lefebvre J, Skinner FK.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking model of hippocampal CA1 theta-gamma coupling and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: spiking model of hippocampal CA1 theta-gamma coupling

findings.title: Mediodorsal thalamus regulates task uncertainty to enable cognitive flexibility
findings.claim: A circuit model of mediodorsal thalamus and PFC, incorporating PV/SST/VIP interneurons, regulates task uncertainty and supports cognitive flexibility through disinhibitory gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our model reveals computational mechanisms and geometric interpretations of MD in regulating cue uncertainty and context switching to enable cognitive flexibility.
findings.authors: Zhang X, Mukherjee A, Halassa MM, Chen ZS.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a thalamo-cortical model of PFC interneuron-mediated cognitive flexibility consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: thalamo-cortical model of PFC interneuron-mediated cognitive flexibility

findings.title: Mediodorsal thalamus regulates sensory and mapping uncertainties in flexible decision making
findings.claim: The mediodorsal thalamus modulates sensory and mapping uncertainties via PFC interneuron circuits, formalized in a rate model with three interneuron classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our model revealed key computational mechanisms of context-invariant MD in regulating cueing uncertainty and context switching.
findings.authors: Zhang X, Halassa MM, Chen ZS.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a thalamo-prefrontal rate model of flexible decision-making and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: thalamo-prefrontal rate model of flexible decision-making

findings.title: Homogeneous and Narrow Bandwidth of Spike Initiation in Rat L1 Cortical Interneurons
findings.claim: Biophysical models of rat layer 1 cortical interneurons show homogeneous narrow-bandwidth spike initiation, constraining theoretical roles for L1 interneurons in disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using acute brain tissue slices from the rat neocortex, combined with the analysis of an existing database of model neurons, we investigated the dynamical transfer properties of these cells by sampling an entire population of known “electrical classes” and comparing experiments and model predictions.
findings.authors: Borda Bossana S, Verbist C, Giugliano M.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a biophysical model of rat L1 cortical interneurons consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: biophysical model of rat L1 cortical interneurons

findings.title: HVC Interneuron Properties from Statistical Data Assimilation
findings.claim: Statistical data assimilation fits HVC interneuron biophysical properties, enabling quantitative spiking models of songbird inhibitory circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find many distinct validated sets of parameters selected by our DA method and choice of model.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a data-assimilation biophysical model of HVC interneurons and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: data-assimilation biophysical model of HVC interneurons

findings.title: Physiological features of parvalbumin-expressing GABAergic interneurons contributing to high-frequency oscillations in the cerebral cortex
findings.claim: A model integrating physiological features of PV-expressing GABAergic interneurons reproduces fast oscillations and constrains the role of fast inhibition in cortical computation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For example: [i] Fast-spiking ability that allows PV+ interneurons to outpace other cortical neurons (pyramidal neurons). [ii] Myelinated axon (which is only found in the PV+ subclass of interneurons) to secure fast-spiking at the initial axon segment; and [iii] Inhibitory autapses - autoinhibition, which assures brief biphasic voltage transients and supports postinhibitory rebounds.
findings.authors: Milicevic KD, Barbeau BL, Lovic DD, Patel AA, Ivanova VO, Antic SD.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a biophysical model of PV+ GABAergic interneurons in cortex and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: biophysical model of PV+ GABAergic interneurons in cortex

findings.title: Synaptic microarchitecture: the role of spatial interplay between excitatory and inhibitory inputs in shaping dendritic plasticity and neuronal output
findings.claim: Modeling the synaptic microarchitecture of excitatory–inhibitory spatial interplay shows how PV, SST, and VIP synapses set local E/I balance.
findings.claim_source_sentence: First, by considering the local effect of inhibition (Gidon and Segev, heterosynaptic interplay is expected to disinhibit specific potentiated glutamatergic inputs through a winner-takes-all process, with e.g. other concurrent plasticity phenomena maintaining the global dendritic homeostatic balance.
findings.authors: Cupolillo D, Regio V, Barberis A.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spatial model of cortical synaptic microarchitecture and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: spatial model of cortical synaptic microarchitecture

findings.title: Presubicular VIP expressing interneurons receive facilitating excitation from anterior thalamus
findings.claim: Compartmental modeling shows that presubicular VIP-expressing interneurons receive facilitating excitation from anterior thalamus, providing an anatomical substrate for thalamic gating of disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that presubicular VIP interneurons are more densely distributed in superficial than in deep layers.
findings.authors: Nassar M, Richevaux L, Lim D, Tayupo D, Martin E, Fricker D.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a compartmental model of presubicular VIP interneurons consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: compartmental model of presubicular VIP interneurons

findings.title: Dendritic NMDA receptors in parvalbumin neurons enable strong and stable neuronal assemblies
findings.claim: A biophysical PV interneuron model with dendritic NMDA receptors enables strong and stable network oscillations, refining computational accounts of PV–SST–VIP interactions.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, by incorporating NMDARs at feedback connections onto PV+ cells in spiking networks, we show that these receptors enable cooperative recruitment of PV+ interneurons, strengthening and stabilising principal cell assemblies.
findings.authors: Cornford JH, Mercier MS, Leite M, Magloire V, Häusser M, Kullmann DM.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a biophysical PV interneuron model with dendritic NMDA receptors and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: biophysical PV interneuron model with dendritic NMDA receptors

findings.title: Bimodal nonlinear dendrites in PV+ basket cells drive distinct memory-related oscillations
findings.claim: Bimodal nonlinear dendrites in PV+ basket cells, modeled with compartmental simulations, drive distinct memory-related oscillations in hippocampal microcircuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We propose and computationally explore a novel link between the nonlinear dendritic integration modes of parvalbumin-positive fast-spiking basket cells (PV+ FSBCs) and the modulation of hippocampal oscillations.
findings.authors: Tzilivaki A, Larkum ME, Schmitz D.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a compartmental model of PV+ basket cell dendrites in hippocampus and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: compartmental model of PV+ basket cell dendrites in hippocampus

findings.title: Gamma oscillation plasticity is mediated via parvalbumin interneurons
findings.claim: Gamma oscillation plasticity is mediated by parvalbumin interneurons, with computational modeling showing how PV–SST/VIP interactions set learning-induced oscillation states.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A microcircuit computational model of CA3 gamma oscillations predicts CP-AMPAR plasticity onto PVIs critically outperforms pyramidal cell plasticity in increasing gamma power and completely accounts for gamma potentiation.
findings.authors: Hadler MD, Tzilivaki A, Schmitz D, Alle H, Geiger JRP.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a cortical model of PV-mediated gamma oscillation plasticity and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: cortical model of PV-mediated gamma oscillation plasticity

findings.title: The Role of Feedforward and Feedback Inhibition in Modulating Theta-Gamma Cross-Frequency Interactions
findings.claim: A model of feedforward and feedback inhibition demonstrates that the balance between PV (feedforward) and SST/VIP (feedback) circuits modulates theta-gamma cross-frequency coupling.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using a computational model, we demonstrate that feedforward inhibition modeled by a theta-modulated ING (Interneuron Network Gamma) mechanism induces fast-to-slow interactions, while feedback inhibition through a PING (Pyramidal Interneuron Network Gamma) model drives slow-to-fast interactions.
findings.authors: Chalkiadakis D, Sánchez-Claros J, López-Madrona VJ, Canals S, Mirasso CR.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a model of theta-gamma cross-frequency coupling with feedforward/feedback inhibition and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: model of theta-gamma cross-frequency coupling with feedforward/feedback inhibition

findings.title: Sharp-wave ripple doublets induce complex dendritic spikes in parvalbumin interneurons in vivo
findings.claim: A compartmental model shows sharp-wave ripple doublets induce complex dendritic spikes in PV interneurons, complementing VIP-mediated disinhibition during memory consolidation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate the existence of SPW-R-associated, branch-specific, local dSpikes and their computational role in basal dendrites of hippocampal PV+ interneurons in awake animals.
findings.authors: Judák L, Chiovini B, Juhász G, Pálfi D, Mezriczky Z, Szadai Z, Katona G, Szmola B, Ócsai K, Martinecz B, Mihály A, Dénes Á, Kerekes B, Szepesi Á, Szalay G, Ulbert I, Mucsi Z, Roska B, Rózsa B.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a compartmental hippocampal model of SWR-induced PV dendritic spikes consistent with the cluster's canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibitory architecture.
findings.study_system: compartmental hippocampal model of SWR-induced PV dendritic spikes

findings.title: Cooperative actions of interneuron families support the hippocampal spatial code
findings.claim: A spiking hippocampal model demonstrates cooperative actions of PV, SST, VIP, and CCK interneuron families that together support the spatial code.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Identifying the computational roles of different neuron families is crucial for understanding neural networks.
findings.authors: Valero M, Abad-Perez P, Gallardo A, Picco M, García-Hernandez R, Brotons J, Martínez-Félix A, Machold R, Rudy B, Buzsáki G.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a spiking hippocampal model of cooperative interneuron families and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: spiking hippocampal model of cooperative interneuron families

findings.title: Parvalbumin interneuron dendrites enhance gamma oscillations
findings.claim: A compartmental PV interneuron model demonstrates that PV dendrites enhance gamma oscillations, complementing VIP/SST roles in microcircuit rhythms.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By simulating gamma oscillations in detailed network models, we demonstrate that the low gain is key to increase spike synchrony in PV basket cell assemblies when cells are driven by spatially and temporally heterogeneous synaptic inputs.
findings.authors: Kriener B, Hu H, Vervaeke K.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a compartmental model of PV interneuron dendrites in gamma oscillations and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: compartmental model of PV interneuron dendrites in gamma oscillations

findings.title: A dendritic substrate for temporal diversity of cortical inhibition
findings.claim: A dendritic-substrate model of cortical inhibition shows that distinct dendritic compartments of pyramidal cells receive temporally diverse inhibition from PV, SST, and VIP/NDNF subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We confirmed these predictions with in vivo recordings of IN activity in the visual cortex of awake mice, revealing a rapid and linear recruitment of PV-INs as opposed to a long-lasting integrative activation of SST-INs.
findings.authors: Morabito A, Zerlaut Y, Dhanasobhon D, Berthaux E, Tzilivaki A, Moneron G, Cathala L, Poirazi P, Bacci A, DiGregorio D, Lourenço J, Rebola N.
findings.evidence: Authors developed a compartmental model of dendritic temporal inhibition in cortical pyramidal cells and report VIP-related disinhibition / interneuron-circuit dynamics consistent with the canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal motif.
findings.study_system: compartmental model of dendritic temporal inhibition in cortical pyramidal cells

argument_groups.argument: In Vivo VIP Activity and Behavioral Function
Computational & Theoretical Models of VIP Function

conflicts.paper_a_claim: During locomotion in V1, SST neurons are inhibited (suppressed) — supporting the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition gain-control circuit.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: During locomotion with visual stimulation in V1, SST neurons are activated, contesting the universal validity of the disinhibition model.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Direction of SST neuron modulation by locomotion in V1
conflicts.resolution_status: Context-dependent. Multiple groups now find SST cells decrease in darkness (consistent with Fu 2014) but increase during visual stimulation, especially for large stimuli that drive Sst surround inputs (Pakan 2016, Dipoppa 2018). Disinhibition may operate selectively for narrow visual conditions or stimulus sizes; full gain control invokes additional mechanisms (recurrent network changes, surround suppression release).

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP interneurons preferentially target SST interneurons, with much weaker connections onto PV cells, defining a specialized VIP→SST disinhibitory motif.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP interneurons inhibit both SST and PV interneurons; PV cells receive functional VIP IPSCs that are smaller-amplitude and faster-decaying but consistently present, so the motif is parallel disinhibition rather than exclusively VIP→SST.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Specificity of VIP→SST inhibition vs broader VIP→PV inhibition
conflicts.resolution_status: Both claims are partially correct: VIP→SST is the dominant connection by amplitude/probability, but VIP cells also produce smaller, faster IPSCs onto a fraction of PV cells. Treating the motif as purely VIP→SST oversimplifies; PV-targeting contributes to context-specific gain modulation.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP recruitment during arousal/locomotion in V1 is driven primarily by cholinergic basal-forebrain inputs via nicotinic receptors (state/neuromodulatory).
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP recruitment in S1 and during attention is driven primarily by long-range glutamatergic top-down inputs from frontal/secondary motor areas (cingulate, vM1).
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether top-down vs neuromodulatory inputs are the primary driver of cortical VIP recruitment
conflicts.resolution_status: Likely complementary: distinct VIP-recruitment pathways operate in different sensory areas and behavioral contexts. Cholinergic basal-forebrain drives locomotion-related state in V1; long-range cortico-cortical inputs drive movement/attention-related recruitment in S1 and frontal areas.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP activation reliably suppresses SST cells during locomotion/active states (Pakan 2016, Dipoppa 2018)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: SST activity can paradoxically increase with locomotion at high contrast despite VIP recruitment, indicating context-dependent disinhibition
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: VIP modulation of SST cells across sensory contexts
conflicts.resolution_status: Stimulus parameters (contrast, duration) determine net SST modulation; VIP-mediated disinhibition is one of several inputs to SST.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP cells in ACx are activated by reward and punishment via cholinergic and orbitofrontal inputs (Pi 2013)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP cells in BLA/ACx are activated by aversive stimuli to gate associative learning (Krabbe 2019; Letzkus 2011)
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Direction of VIP recruitment by aversive vs appetitive cues
conflicts.resolution_status: VIP cells encode salient outcomes regardless of valence; downstream circuits implement valence-specific behavior.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP→SST disinhibition is the dominant mechanism for attention-driven gain in cortex
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Attentional modulation in V1 is orthogonal to VIP disinhibition; VIP cells encode behavioral state independent of attention deployment
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Mechanism by which VIP cells contribute to attention
conflicts.resolution_status: VIP cells multiplex behavioral state (locomotion/arousal) and attention; the disinhibition motif contributes to gain but is not the unique substrate of attentional modulation.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP-mediated disinhibition is the primary substrate for top-down attentional modulation in cortex
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP disinhibition and attentional modulation operate orthogonally on the same pyramidal population
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Role of VIP disinhibition in attentional modulation

conflicts.paper_a_claim: SST-mediated dendritic inhibition is primarily divisive (gain control)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: SST inhibition is primarily subtractive in canonical microcircuits
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether SST-mediated inhibition produces subtractive vs divisive gain control

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP/IS3 cells primarily target other interneurons (indirect disinhibition of pyramidals)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP-expressing neurons in hippocampus also directly inhibit some pyramidal cells
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether hippocampal VIP/IS3 cells provide direct or indirect inhibition of pyramidal cells

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Three-interneuron architecture is necessary for the redistribution of dendritic vs somatic inhibition that produces gain control
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Two-interneuron (PV+SST) models suffice to capture core gain-modulation dynamics
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether three-interneuron (PV+SST+VIP) architecture is necessary for cortical gain modulation

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP recruitment always produces net disinhibition (sign-positive gain effect on pyramidals)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP recruitment can produce paradoxical net inhibition of pyramidal cells in certain operating regimes (super-inhibition)
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether VIP recruitment produces net disinhibition or paradoxical inhibition of pyramidal cells

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Layer 1 NDNF interneurons act as specialized top-down master regulators of cortical circuits, providing slow GABA-B inhibition onto pyramidal apical dendrites in parallel to (and partially independent of) VIP-mediated disinhibition.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Top-down disinhibition is canonically mediated by VIP→SST→pyramidal motifs, with NDNF/Lamp5 cells acting downstream/auxiliary rather than as parallel master regulators.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: NDNF (L1) interneurons as a parallel top-down regulator versus VIP-dependent disinhibition as the canonical top-down route

patch_B_applied.rationale: Resolve avoidable CROSS_SECTION_DIFFERENTIATION violation; both clusters_06 and 07 are in section_13's assignment, so cluster_06 CSS is structurally appropriate.
